solution: The technician found the tank monitor (ATG) was in reboot mode. They powered off the monitor, waited 2 minutes, then powered it back on, which restored normal operation to 100%. The site manager confirmed the tank monitor was displaying properly after the restart.

problem: One of the two Ruby registers was displaying a maintenance required error on its pinpad.
solution: The technician replaced the pinpad on register 102 with a new MX915 pinpad and tested both chip and tap functions with a debit card to verify operation.

problem: Dispenser would not shut off after dispensing fuel.
solution: Technician diagnosed a faulty electronic reset module that was staying on. Replaced the electronic reset module with a new one and tested the system to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 8 dispenser was not dispensing any product and showed a remote diagnostic error.
solution: The technician reset and reloaded the jade board in fueling point 8, then tested the dispenser to confirm it was working.

problem: Fueling point 8 card reader had debris stuck inside, preventing cards from being inserted and taking the fueling point offline.
solution: Technician replaced the card reader on fueling point 8. The technician also replaced the nozzles on fueling points 6 and 8 per the site's request, then tested and monitored all affected fueling points with no errors found.
parts: 2x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling point 6 was declining all debit and credit transactions despite showing no error codes.
solution: The technician swapped the Secure Card Device between fueling points 5 and 6 to rule out a faulty pinpad, then re-injected debit encryption keys on the SPM 2.5 and rebooted the dispenser. When the issue persisted, they performed an iXReset and debug on the Jade board, reloaded the latest iXPay software, reconfigured the Jade board, and rebooted the dispenser to allow full system load. Debit and credit transactions were tested and confirmed working properly.

problem: Fueling point 4 display was showing a black screen and not responding.
solution: Technician performed an iXReset and debug on the Jade board, which restored communication with the application. The latest iXPay software was reloaded, the board was configured, the system was rebooted, and testing confirmed proper operation with a successful test transaction.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Fueling point 4 unihose experienced a drive-off incident and the fueling point was out of service; fueling points 4, 6, and 10 needed firmware updates and configuration.
solution: Technician loaded Idpos firmware on fueling points 10 and 6, configured and tested both fueling points, and replaced the breakaway on fueling point 4. All fueling points were tested and verified operational. No labor or trip charges were applied due to the previous technician's failure to perform the firmware upgrade.

problem: The automatic tank gauge (ATG) was offline and needed to be rebooted.
solution: The technician powered off the tank monitor for 2 minutes, then powered it back on, which restored full functionality. The technician called the site contact to confirm the tank gauge was back online.

problem: The electronic leak detectors (ELLDs) on the diesel and premium submersible turbine pump sumps failed to shut down when a leak was simulated during testing.
solution: Replaced the electronic leak detectors with mechanical leak detectors on both the diesel and premium sumps. After replacement, the technician bypassed the electronic leak detectors and tested all systems, which then returned to 100% pass on the tank monitor.

problem: Fueling point 4 was cutting off mid-sale with a malfunctioning electronic product display board showing a solid green light instead of flashing normally, and fueling point 3 was dispensing slowly on all products.
solution: Replaced the malfunctioning board in fueling point 4 and adjusted DIP switches, but the replacement board was also defective. Swapped the electronic product display from fueling point 3 to fueling point 4 and adjusted DIP switches; fueling point 4 now dispenses both regular unleaded and premium at 8 GPM. A return visit is needed to install a new electronic product display for fueling point 3 and diagnose the slow flow issue.

problem: The diesel submersible turbine pump probe was not reading correctly, causing low product alarms.
solution: Replaced the faulty probe after troubleshooting the submersible turbine pump, probe, and floats. Advised the customer that debris in the bottom of the tank should be removed to ensure accurate readings going forward.

problem: All diesel pumps stopped working on the dispenser.
solution: Technician tested the submersible turbine pump system and found the relay worn and needing replacement, and discovered the pipe holding the submersible turbine pump was broken at the head. Replaced the submersible turbine pump head, pipe, and pigtail connector to restore diesel flow.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP 3/4 HP Pump Motor Asse [400276902] ; 1x STEM WIRING HARNESS

problem: Fueling point 5 universal hose nozzle and hoses were disconnected due to a breakaway coupling failure, leaving the pump out of service.
solution: Technician reattached both the nozzle and hoses to the breakaway coupling on pumps 5 and 7, tested the connection, and observed customer use to verify proper operation. No parts were replaced.

problem: The hose on the right dispenser was leaking at the breakaway connection.
solution: The technician found the hose connection to the breakaway was loose. They removed the hose, reapplied pipe seal, and tightened the connection back together. The leak was resolved and the dispenser returned to service without further leaking.

problem: Fueling point 8 regular dispenser had an intermittent leak at the nozzle-hose connection, specifically at the swivel.
solution: Technician replaced the swivel on fueling point 8 and tested all functions to confirm the leak was resolved and normal operation was restored.

problem: Regular unleaded and premium fuel grades were assigned to the wrong fueling points, and both MX915 pin pads were displaying the Shell logo instead of the correct branding.
solution: Corrected the logical nozzle position configurations in Configuration Manager from positions 1, 2, and 3 to the correct positions 3, 5, and 7 for regular, plus, and premium grades, then initialized the fuel system and verified all fuel grades and pricing were properly mapped. Reloaded the latest ViperPay software on both MX915 pin pads to remove the Shell logo and restore correct branding, then tested both pin pads to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 6 hose was leaking at the breakaway coupling.
solution: The technician tightened the loose swivel and replaced the leaking nozzle. After repairs, the fueling point was tested and monitored to confirm all issues were resolved.

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser screen was blank and unresponsive.
solution: Diagnosed that the Red board was obsolete and unrepairable. Replaced the Red board with a new Jade board, loaded the latest iXPay software, configured the system, initialized DCRs and EMV readers, and verified operation with a successful test transaction. Fueling point 6 is now back online and operating properly.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Fueling point 6 keypad was not available and fueling point 7 screen was black.
solution: Replaced the SPM (sales and payment module) on fueling point 6 and reset and reloaded the Jade board on fueling point 7. All systems tested and functioning properly. Note that the SPM installed on fueling point 6 is an APEC brand part still under warranty from October 2025 installation.

problem: After a power outage, fueling positions 1 through 4 on the diesel dispenser went offline with gray background and white lines on the register display, and the nozzle auto shutoff on fueling position 6 was not functioning due to a missing internal spring.
solution: Technician found that the iGEM configuration had reverted to default settings due to a low internal battery, and remotely reprogrammed the iGEM and reinitialize the fuel system to bring fueling positions 1 through 4 back online. The nozzle on fueling position 6 was replaced due to the missing spring, tested for proper operation with no leaks observed.

problem: Regular unleaded fuel dispenser was not operating.
solution: Replaced the bad motor, wiring harness, and yoke. Tested the dispenser and confirmed all functions working as expected.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Lead Assembly, 156" [151213930] ; 1x FFS-FEP Wire connector kit (In [400200930] ; 1x FFS-FEP 2 HP Variable Speed Pu [400276905]

problem: Fueling point 3 was offline with an error message on the display, and fueling point 1 was not reading payment cards.
solution: For fueling point 3, the technician reloaded and reconfigured the Jade board, then verified the dispenser came back online and completed a successful test transaction. For fueling point 1, the technician ran diagnostics and tested the card reader, confirming it properly reads both EMV chip and magstripe cards.

problem: Fueling point 4 was cutting off mid-sale due to a malfunctioning electronic product display board, fueling point 3 was dispensing fuel slowly, fueling point 3 premium fuel was not dispensing, and fueling point 7 nozzle was leaking at the spout base.
solution: Replaced the malfunctioning electronic product display board on fueling point 4 and swapped the display from fueling point 3 to fueling point 4 to restore operation. On the return trip, installed a new electronic product display on fueling point 3, replaced both grade filters that had not been changed since March 2025 to address slow flow, and replaced the pump relay board on the premium circuit which was showing 0 volts. Replaced the leaking nozzle on fueling point 7. All fueling points now dispense properly at correct flow rates with no leaks.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120] ; 1x 7685                      RDM New Style Pump Relay Board ; 1x Wayne Display Board, Programme [WU020250-0001]

problem: Fueling point 5 dispenser was stuck displaying error E01 (please pay inside) and would not come online; the backlight board was defective.
solution: Replaced the backlight board on fueling point 5, rebooted the Commander, Wayne Connect, and FCI systems. Fueling point 5 DCR came back online, the E01 error cleared, and pay-at-the-pump and prepay functions were tested and confirmed working.

problem: Fueling point 9 was offline from the network and displaying an 'Out of Service' message due to a breached secure payment module keypad.
solution: Replaced the breached secure payment module keypad, injected debit encryption, and reloaded the Jade board to update firmware for the new keypad. The dispenser was returned to operational mode, allowed to fully initialize, and a successful test transaction confirmed it is now operating normally.

problem: The fuel dispenser master unit was not receiving any power.
solution: The technician tested the fuses and electrical connections (all were good), then reset the power distribution unit which restored power to the dispenser. Manual and stand-alone operation were verified, and a test transaction with a card was completed successfully to confirm full operation.

problem: Fueling point 12 DCR screen was not responding to any input; intermittent issues reported on dispensers using Anthem Open Architecture.
solution: Technician arrived on site and tested the equipment without finding any issues. The problem was likely caused by a Sam's Club credit card locking the system. Recommend monitoring for recurrence of this intermittent locking issue.

problem: Customer-provided Ruby2 point-of-sale register needed to be installed and tested as station register 102.
solution: Technician installed power adapter for the Ruby2 terminal, tested the MX915 pinpad with debit card transactions to confirm functionality, and verified the customer display was working. Technician noted that the Ruby2 system lacks a built-in printer and scanner, which means no printed receipts for customers and manual entry required for all items during checkout.

problem: Fueling point 10 was offline from the network with a stuck sale halting the pump.
solution: Technician called the site contact to confirm the issue, arrived on site, and tested fueling point 10. The pump was found to be fully operational after testing, indicating the stuck sale condition had cleared.

problem: The tank monitor probe was not reading any fuel level data from the tank.
solution: Replaced the premium probe with a DM probe, which is the correct probe type for this installation. Also replaced the probe and monitor cap at the tank to accommodate the new probe. After installation, verified all programming on the pro gauge was correct and confirmed the system is functioning properly with email alerts now being received.
parts: 1x OPW NEW FMS Riser Cap [FM30-0229] ; 1x OPW-NEW-FMS Float Kit-Gasoline [FM30-1516-02] ; 1x OPW-NEW FMS PROBE,RIGID [FM30-DMP101]

problem: Dispenser pump 2 displayed a KSB error, possibly indicating a failed submersible turbine pump.
solution: Removed the failed submersible turbine pump and installed a newer model unit. Tested all functions and confirmed normal operation.
Not stated

problem: Fueling points 1 and 4 were displaying a KSB error, caused by a breached SPM keypad.
solution: The technician replaced the SPM keypad and properly secured it. Debit encryption was injected, the unit was placed into operational mode, and testing was performed successfully.

problem: Fueling point 6 was offline from the network and unable to process card transactions.
solution: Reloaded the Jade board and SCD with the latest IDPOS software, configured the Jade board, and rebooted the dispenser. After allowing fueling point 6 to fully come online, testing confirmed it was back online and processing card transactions properly.

problem: Fueling point 10 on the dispenser was not functioning and displaying error code 8 on the customer display; fueling points 1 and 2 were also bagged off and not operational.
solution: The technician reseated the cabling connections on fueling point 10, which restored the customer display and fueling function. Fueling point 2 was reactivated by another technician, and the fueling point 1 payment processing unit that was malfunctioning was swapped with a working unit, restoring operation to both fueling points.

problem: The EMV chip reader on the card reader was not reading customer payment cards.
solution: The technician ran diagnostics and found the EMV software had not been refreshed when the SPM (secure payment module) was previously replaced. The technician refreshed the EMV and retested all functions, which then operated normally.

problem: The card reader on fueling point 1 was not responding and showing a red solid light.
solution: Replaced the card reader on fueling point 1 and cleaned corrosion off both sides of the equipment. Paired and replaced one sensor unit on fueling points 1 and 2, then tested all functions and confirmed with Wayne tech support that the repair was successful.
parts: 1x WAYNE Anthem Card Reader [W2894103-002] ; 1x WAYNE MODULE, DFS WIRELESS IN- [WU019855-0001]

problem: Premium fuel probe was not communicating with the tank level sensor after CPU installation, causing an invalid fuel level error with 20 gallons of water detected.
solution: Technician identified that the premium probe needed to be replaced with a MAG 109 probe. Dealer was contacted to authorize the replacement; technician is awaiting customer approval to proceed with the probe replacement.

problem: Fueling point 1 unihose nozzle and swivel were leaking fuel, and the dispenser door screen was loose due to broken plastic clips.
solution: Replaced the leaking hose and swivel with new components and tested for proper operation. Replaced the dispenser door with a used door from another site after transferring all electronics from the original door, then tested all functions to confirm proper operation.

problem: Dispenser Commander was displaying error status 40 with no communication to the Gilbarco dispenser electronics, causing the register and pump remote service to be down.
solution: Replaced the Commander unit using the current database. After installation, the Commander came back online successfully and all fueling points were initialized and verified operating properly.

problem: KSB error displayed on fueling points 5, 6, and 8 due to bad Secured Payment Modules (SPMs).
solution: Replaced the defective SPMs on fueling points 5, 6, and 8 with new units. Loaded Chevron encryption keys to all three SPMs and tested all three fueling points. When a replaced SPM on fueling point 6 was found to be breached on the return visit, it was replaced again under warranty. Also replaced a breakaway on fueling point 3.

problem: Non-ethanol fueling point has a leak in the tank, and pump #2 display screen is blank.
solution: Technician attempted to install an O-ring but was unable to complete the fill leads installation due to cold weather conditions. Dealer will complete the O-ring installation with silicone sealant when weather warms. Per dealer direction, the screen issue was not addressed and no filters were replaced at this time.
parts: 4x OPW 5 Gallon Cover Seal for us [H12229M]

problem: A pinpad at the register was not working.
solution: The technician replaced the pinpad on register 101, loaded and injected it, then tested it with a debit card to verify functionality.

problem: Pumps 3 and 4 were displaying a COIPA error due to the in-dispenser wireless unit losing communication with the access point.
solution: The technician refreshed the wireless unit's synchronization with the access point, which restored normal communication and cleared the error.

problem: Fueling point 1 autoshutoff was engaging prematurely, preventing customers from fueling, and the hold-open latch was sticking; fueling point 10 was offline and behaving abnormally.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 1 and purged the system without issues. For fueling point 10, technician wiped and reloaded the IX Jade system, which restored normal and fully functional operation.

problem: Fueling point 12 unihose was reported as leaking at the swivel, with the leak being intermittent depending on nozzle position.
solution: Technician arrived and inspected fueling point 12, finding all components functioning properly. Confirmed with site staff that no issues were currently present. No corrective action was required.

problem: Fueling points 1 through 4 were dispensing at slow flow rates, with regular unleaded at 4 GPM and premium at 7.5 GPM.
solution: Technician installed a flow gauge to diagnose the issue and replaced the regular unleaded filters on all fueling points. After filter replacement, regular unleaded flow increased to 8 GPM with no leaks observed. Premium fuel flow rate was not re-tested after the repair.

problem: Fueling point 8 regular nozzle swivel was leaking during use, causing the fueling point to be out of service.
solution: Replaced the leaking swivel on fueling point 8 and purged air from the system. All functions tested normal and auto-shutoff works as intended. Fueling points 6 and 10 were also inspected and found to have no issues.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were offline; fueling points 6 and 10 had unresponsive buttons on their dispensers.
solution: Replaced the jade board on fueling point 1 and reloaded both jade and SCD boards. Replaced the IGEM (which had lost its functions and was causing recurring issues after power outages), bootstrapped it to version 86.00, loaded the template, and programmed functions, bringing both fueling points back online. Replaced the buttons on fueling points 6 and 10.
parts: 1x RDM CPU Board (WM001908-R003) ; 2x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK ; 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Register 2 pinpad was displaying a blue screen and tap-to-pay transactions were declining, while the site was also experiencing issues with multiple fueling points.
solution: Reloaded and upgraded the pinpad, ran diagnostics, and contacted Chevron Helpdesk to determine that the Commander software version (Base 53) had tap-to-pay disabled as a fraud prevention measure. Customer approved a software upgrade to enable tap-to-pay functionality. Additionally, reloaded both Ruby 2 registers and the Commander interface, and repaired fueling points by reconnecting disconnected Wayne Connect cables on fueling points 3 and 7, and restoring fueling point 5 via iXReset on the Jade board. Fueling points 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 require hardware replacement on a return visit. Recommend completing the Commander software upgrade and scheduling replacement of breached SPM keypads and Jade boards.

problem: The TLS-350 dispenser displayed a 'wrong software warning' and was not reading correctly; the CPU board was determined to be corrupted.
solution: Replaced the CPU board with kit 331500-308 and reprogrammed it using the printout found inside the unit. All systems returned to normal operation. Technician noted that the site had a recent fuel delivery and the ATG register is showing 6 inches more than expected; recommend returning to perform a manual tank stick to verify inventory.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 6 diesel nozzle swivel was leaking during use, and the gas nozzle was not shutting off properly, causing fuel spills; both gas and diesel fueling points were out of service.
solution: The technician replaced both defective swivel joints on the hanging hardware assemblies, replaced the gas nozzle (the return spring beneath the clip lever was missing), replaced the 8-foot hose due to kinks, and replaced both gas and diesel OPW breakaway devices with Catlow breakaways. All connections were secured, lines were purged, and leak testing confirmed no leaks remained; both fueling points were returned to service.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 2x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x ; 2x CENTRAL Breakaway, ", Catlow

problem: Fueling points 3, 4, 9, and 10 intermittently freeze on the 'Please Remove Card' screen multiple times per day, requiring system resets to restore operation.
solution: Technician tested EMV chip card readers on the affected fueling points and confirmed they process transactions successfully with no active errors observed. The issue appears to be an intermittent EMV transaction completion hang; recommend monitoring for recurrence and contacting support if freezing continues, as the problem could not be reproduced during the service visit.

problem: A customer's credit card was stuck in the card reader at fueling point 5 because the card was dented and wedged inside.
solution: The technician used a screwdriver to carefully pry the damaged card out of the reader, then tested the card reader to confirm all functions were normal.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were not dispensing product; reboot did not resolve the issue.
solution: Technician inspected error codes and found premium shear valve was closed. Opened the valve and tested all fuel grades, confirming all systems were functional.

problem: Diesel product was not displaying in the daily end-of-day report, even though it was present in the system.
solution: Checked Commander configurations and found no local issues. Contacted Verifone Helpdesk support, who remotely removed the Manual Fuel department from the system. The site was advised to review the next end-of-day report to verify that Diesel displays correctly. (Verifone Ticket #13075768)

problem: All fueling points were declining credit cards at the register pinpad and pump card readers.
solution: Upgraded the customer interface software to version 1.17.05 with a two-step process and tested transactions both at the register and at the dispensers to confirm they were working. Customer was instructed to install a submersible turbine pump later and confirmation was obtained before dispatch.

problem: Fueling points 6 and 7 were displaying 'card unavailable' error messages and were out of service despite the site's attempts to reset and reboot the dispensers.
solution: The technician arrived on site and found no actual issues with the card readers; all fueling points were back online. Transactions were tested with the attendant and customer usage was observed with no further issues detected.
Not stated

problem: Tank drain plug, vent valve cap, and droptube gasket needed replacement.
solution: Replaced the tank's drain plug, vent cap, and O-ring beneath the droptube as recommended by the compliance team. Site requires retesting for environmental vapor recovery (EVR) compliance in a separate service call.
parts: 1x OPW Replacement Drain Plug Kit ; 1x OPW Pressure / Vacuum Vent, 2" ; 1x OPW O-Ring, Drop Tube Seal, fo

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose was leaking at the breakaway connector, and fueling points 1, 7, and 11 were reported out of service with various issues including authorization/dispensing failures and card reader malfunction.
solution: Replaced the breakaway connector on fueling point 11 and replaced the nozzle on fueling point 6, then tested both. Upon arrival, technician tested fueling points 7 and 8 and found no problems; fueling point 1 also tested with no issues found.

problem: Fueling points 1, 2, 3, and 4 were continuously rebooting.
solution: Technician found that fueling points 1 and 2 had breached surge protection modules which are not covered under the IOTV contract warranty. For fueling points 3 and 4, the technician power cycled the display units which resolved the rebooting issue.

problem: Fueling point 11 with IXJade system was locked up and frozen, preventing normal operation.
solution: Technician wiped and reloaded fueling point 11, then tested all functions to confirm normal operation was restored.

problem: Fueling point 3 was reported to be overflowing when preset amount was reached.
solution: Technician tested both fuel grades on fueling point 3 and found no issue present at the time of service.

problem: Fueling points 1 through 4 were greyed out and offline on the register, causing all pumps to appear unavailable.
solution: Verified the current loop communication between the register and dispensers was functioning properly, then rebooted both the Topaz and Commander systems. After rebooting, all fueling points returned online and were confirmed operational and accepting prepay transactions.

problem: All fueling points were down with no communication between the dispenser and the front-end controller.
solution: The technician identified a blown port in the commander unit and replaced it. After replacing the port, fuel was reset and both prepay and credit card transactions were tested and confirmed working properly.

problem: Site received incorrect tank lid seal covers; only one of the four ordered lids (orange vapor recovery lid) was the correct fit for the equipment.
solution: Technician installed the one correct orange vapor recovery lid on site. Site needs to reorder one additional orange vapor recovery lid for the other regular vapor tube, and OPW-1C series lids for regular (white), premium (red), and diesel (yellow) applications, as the original lids provided do not fit.
The technician reloaded and reconfigured the IDPOS system on fueling point 4, then tested it to verify operation.

problem: All fueling points were displaying an error message and not accepting transactions, and fueling point 3 was not allowing prepay transactions.
solution: The technician power-cycled the Wayne Connect 485 communication unit and rebooted the Commander system to perform a full system refresh. After the reboot completed and all units reloaded, a test transaction was performed successfully and fueling point 3 prepay functionality was confirmed to be working properly.

problem: Fueling point 1 nozzle was defective, register 101 touch screen was hanging and unresponsive, and fueling point 1 jade board was displaying errors.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 1, reloaded and reset the jade board with reconfiguration and testing, and reloaded register 101 software to restore touch screen functionality. All systems were tested and verified working.

problem: Fueling points 3, 4, 11, and 12 were out of service with card readers not accepting cards.
solution: Fueling points 11 and 12 were already repaired under another ticket as a warranty issue. Fueling points 3 and 4 were found to be working properly upon arrival. All fueling points are now operational.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were out of service with card readers not accepting cards.
solution: On fueling point 12, power was cycled which resolved the issue. On fueling point 11, the card reader was replaced. Both fueling points were tested and confirmed working.

problem: Touchscreen on register 101 was not working.
solution: Technician reloaded the register software and cycled power to the fueling point, which restored touchscreen functionality. Technician also inspected fueling points 6 and 8 and found keyboard/button issues (KSB), but customer declined replacement at that time.

problem: Fueling point 3 nozzle and swivel assembly were leaking, causing the fueling point to be out of service. Additionally, diesel dispensers 11, 12, 5, and 6 had clogged filters causing low flow rates of only 2 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced the nozzle and swivel assembly on fueling point 3 and tested all functions to 100% operation. Replaced filters on diesel dispensers 11, 12, 5, and 6, restoring diesel flow to the proper 8 to 10 gallons per minute.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 2 EMV keypad was breached, causing a 'Pay Inside' error message, and the Veeder Root tank gauge system was showing incorrect fuel inventory due to stuck and missing tank probes.
solution: Replaced the breached keypad on fueling point 2 to resolve the payment error. The technician was unable to extract the stuck regular probe from its tube and found the premium probe missing, so a return visit is needed with replacement probes and 2-inch float kits to recalibrate the tank gauge system.

problem: Slow fuel flow on all fueling points across all grades at newly installed dispensers.
solution: Technician found air trapped in the fuel system. Had all customers stop pumping, then restarted the system which restored flow to normal operating conditions. Technician recommends changing filters in the future when station traffic is lighter, as station was too busy to perform this maintenance at the time of service.

problem: Fueling point 10 was declining all credit and debit cards despite a restart attempt.
solution: The technician accessed diagnostics on the fueling point and tested both the EMV chip reader and magstripe functions, which both tested successfully. After verifying normal dispenser operation through multiple successful customer transactions, no faults or error conditions were detected, indicating the issue was resolved.

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose breakaway was intermittently leaking during use due to a faulty swivel connection.
solution: Technician repaired the swivel on the breakaway and tested the fueling point to confirm it was operating at full capacity with no leaks.

problem: Tap to pay functionality was not working at any of the eight fueling points at this site.
solution: Replaced all eight tap to pay kits, initialized the DCRs and EMV readers, and tested all systems successfully.

problem: Fueling point 4 display screen was completely cracked and shattered.
solution: Replaced the entire display assembly and reconnected all wiring, then powered the unit back on and verified proper operation. Fueling point 4 is now functioning correctly.

problem: Tank 1 fueling point pumping slow after flex hose replacement by construction; multiple issues found including bad check valve on gasoline, bad leak detector on diesel causing fuel to pour from exit tube, dirty lines with soot buildup, faulty meter, and bad solenoid valve.
solution: Technician replaced the check valve for gasoline, replaced the leak detector for diesel, replaced the filter, and installed a new meter with a new meter check valve after the tank was cleaned. On final visit, replaced the solenoid valve (model G6201D, serial 18075D) and found a leaking flex hose in the transition sump that requires replacement.
parts: 1x DAVIS MECHANICAL METER 2PM6 [046378-D-REB] ; 1x DAVIS MECHANICAL METER 2PM6 [046378-D-REB] ; 1x V/R7296 Reset/Wayne, Refbsh [330001] ; 1x V/R7296 Reset/Wayne, Refbsh [330001] ; 1x FFS-FEP Model "W" Check Valve [400988933] ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70065] ; 1x 99 LD2200                   VAPORLESS Leak Detector

solution: Installed a new C18 point-of-sale system with latest Chevron software and basic template, established network communication through Mako box and switch, programmed Chevron Loyalty Rewards and P97 with receipt verification, and programmed POP settings for Chevron Proprietary Cards with 10-cent discount. Note: ATG installation deferred due to tank riser issues; FastField documentation for dispenser and ATG startup still needed.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were offline with a non-functioning pump screen and printer issue.
solution: Replaced the defective DFS wired Connect in-dispenser unit that could not communicate with the server. Installed a new wired DFS in-dispenser unit and verified proper server communication; fueling points 9 and 10 are now online and operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 12 card reader was reported as not reading cards.
solution: Technician checked the fueling point 12 card reader in diagnostics and verified it can read both EMV chip and magstripe cards. Multiple customers were observed successfully using the card reader during the visit, and no problem was found.

problem: Fueling point 12 main screen was blank and showing offline on the point-of-sale system; rebooting from the breaker did not resolve the issue.
solution: The technician reloaded the IDPOS software on fueling point 12, then configured and tested it to restore operation. Note: One swivel was also replaced on fueling point 3.
The Jade board was reset and reloaded on fueling point 3, then tested to confirm operation.

problem: The Veeder Root TLS 350 tank monitor displayed a 'wrong software warning' and all three product tanks were showing as offline with a dead battery.
solution: The technician performed a cold start and replaced the battery, but the issue persisted. The CPU board was determined to be corrupted and requires replacement with part number 331500-308 to resolve the problem.

problem: Fuel Master system issue on dispenser #2.
solution: Tested the main and satellite dispensers to verify operation, then reset the Fuel Master system. All functions tested and confirmed working as expected.
Not stated

problem: Diesel pump flow rate was too low at approximately 2 GPM, preventing the auto-shutoff from functioning properly.
solution: Replaced the diesel filters which were clogged with debris, restoring flow rate to 8-10 GPM and confirming auto-shutoff functionality on both fueling points. Technician photographed debris and notified the dealer of possible algae contamination in the diesel tank that may require further investigation.

problem: Fueling point 7 stopped dispensing after reaching $10 during the payment and dispensing process due to valve issues on the premium valve.
solution: Technician replaced the check valve and relief valve on fueling point 7, secured all components, and performed multiple pump tests to confirm normal operation. During a follow-up visit, technician also replaced a leaking nozzle on fueling point 5.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x WAYNE Solenoid Valve Proportio ; 1x WAYNE Check Valve and Relief,

problem: Fueling point 15 card reader screen was stuck on 'pump ready' but the dispenser would not dispense fuel.
solution: The technician reconnected the breakaway coupling, which had been disconnected due to a drive-off incident. This restored the dispenser to service.
Not stated

problem: Site requested filter inspection on all fueling points across all fuel grades.
solution: Technician inspected filters on all 10 fueling points and replaced the clogged premium filter at fueling points 1/2 and the clogged regular fuel filter at fueling points 5/6. Remaining fueling points 3/4, 7/8, and 9/10 had no filter issues.

problem: Fueling point 5 printer paper rack was reported as broken.
solution: Technician inspected the fueling point 5 printer rack on arrival and found it to be in good working condition with no issues. Printer was tested and confirmed to be operating normally.

problem: Fueling point #5/6 displayed a KSB error and was blinking in closed status.
solution: The technician performed a cold start on the iGem controller, reloaded and reprogrammed the fueling point, then tested to confirm the error was cleared.

problem: A customer's card became stuck inside the card reader of fueling point 7, preventing the dispenser from operating.
solution: The technician removed the stuck card from the card reader and repaired the card reader. Test transactions were completed successfully to verify the fix.

problem: Premium fueling point motor was locked rotor, causing a motor error and preventing fuel dispensing.
solution: Replaced the locked rotor motor with a new unit and tested it to confirm proper operation. The replacement motor is covered under warranty.

problem: Fueling point 10 unihose was reported leaking at the breakaway connection.
solution: Technician inspected fueling point 10 on arrival and found it functioning normally with no leaks present. The breakaway connection was checked and found to be in good condition.

problem: Fueling point 11 was stuck in a reboot cycle, repeatedly cycling to the QR screen, then white screen, then rebooting, leaving the fueling point out of service.
solution: The technician reloaded the Jade board with SCD firmware on fueling point 11 and reconfigured it. The fueling point was tested and confirmed working.

problem: Fueling point 5 nozzle hold-open latch was reported loose with a contained spill, and the fueling point was out of service.
solution: Upon arrival, the technician found that fueling point 5 was operational with no issues detected. No corrective action was documented.

problem: Register was printing duplicate cash out receipts, and the customer wanted to disable receipt printing.
solution: Disabled print receipt settings for both cash and sales transactions to prevent duplicate receipts. However, Chevron card receipts will still print as they are controlled separately. Also reset the dispensing control unit driver and resent to all fueling points; pumps 8 and 3 have separate issues (breached submersible turbine pump on fueling point 3 and IXJade-related issue on fueling point 8) that the customer declined service for at this time.

problem: Multiple fueling points experiencing slow flow intermittently, with some dispensers offline due to filter pressure issues, frozen sales displays, and a leaking nozzle swivel.
solution: Technician replaced clogged filters on fueling points 9/10 and 1/2 with shop filters, which restored normal flow rates. Power cycled fueling point 7 to clear the frozen sales display. Fueling point 8 nozzle swivel showed no leaks upon inspection and was found to be operating normally. Fueling point 3 was out of service pending price file recovery from the back-office system.

problem: Card was damaged and jammed in the card reader of fueling point 7, causing the dispenser to be down; fueling point 12 card reader was not functional and the dispenser was frozen.
solution: Removed the damaged card from fueling point 7 using a screwdriver as leverage to unjam it. Power cycled fueling point 12 dispenser to resolve the frozen card reader issue. Both fueling points returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 3 were not dispensing any fuel; fueling point 4 satellite was not working; diesel on fueling point 2 was not operating due to a defective relay board; fueling point 3 had a steady leak underneath.
solution: Technician replaced the defective relay board on fueling point 2, which restored diesel dispensing operation. Replaced the filter on fueling point 3 to resolve the leak. For fueling point 4 satellite, initial inspection found a bad relay board and handle switch; after replacement of the relay board, further inspection determined the iGEM board requires reprogramming or replacement, which is pending completion on a return visit.

problem: Fueling point 11 diesel pump was not working and had slow flow compared to other satellite pumps.
solution: Technician tested the pump in standalone mode and with various code sets, then reprogrammed the functions. Found that the relay was sending power to the motor but determined the motor is likely bad or there is corrosion in the junction box that prevented opening it for inspection. Technician recommended bringing 6 filters to the site as a precaution since the units are running slower than normal.

problem: Dispenser doors at fueling points 1-12 required upgrade to AX12 model.
solution: The technician shut down the site, collected MAC addresses from each dispenser door, coordinated with Walmart technical support for proxy server configuration, removed all existing dispenser doors, and installed new AX12 doors following Anthem Open Architecture PXE Installation Instructions v9.1. Multiple test transactions were completed successfully before returning the site to operational status.

problem: Diesel is not dispensing from pump number 9, and the nozzle is leaking.
solution: The technician discovered that the breaker was completely removed from the breaker box. The customer requested to reschedule the service call.

problem: Fueling point 8 displayed an OS version 603 113 boot load error and was out of service after the site attempted a reset/reboot without success.
solution: Loaded and configured Idpos on fueling point 8 and tested the system to verify operation.

problem: Fueling point 7 displayed a 'Pay at Pump Not Available, Use Card Inside' error due to a breached secure PIN pad keypad.
solution: Replaced the affected secure PIN pad keypad, injected the debit encryption key, placed the unit into operational mode, and loaded the dynamic currency routing and EMV configuration from the register. A test transaction was performed and completed successfully.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 8 displayed KSB errors, fueling point 7 had a blank screen, and fueling point 11 was offline with no power.
solution: Technician found the breaker for fueling point 11 had been turned off in the back room breaker panel and reset it, restoring power and bringing fueling point 11 back online. The corrective action notes do not state what was done to resolve the KSB errors on fueling points 1 and 8 or the blank screen on fueling point 7.
Not stated

problem: Premium grade fuel was dispensing slowly at fueling point 5 due to clogged filters restricting fuel flow.
solution: Technician installed a pressure gauge and confirmed low flow rate of 8 GPM caused by filter restrictions. Replaced clogged filters at fueling points 3/4, 5/6, and 7/8, then tested all affected fueling points to confirm normal 8 GPM dispensing with no leaks. Verified the fueling point 5 submersible turbine pump keypad is online and functioning properly. Recommend replacing the remaining filter at fueling point 1/2 when inventory becomes available.

problem: Fueling point 1 diesel nozzle swivel/breakaway assembly was leaking due to partial detachment, and the gasoline nozzle auto shutoff was not functioning with slow fuel flow at approximately 6 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced the defective diesel swivel/breakaway assembly and tested for leaks. Replaced all dispenser filters and installed a new gasoline nozzle, then purged all fuel grades and performed operational testing to confirm all grades dispensed properly. Both nozzle and swivel breakaway replacement are covered under warranty.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW 3/4" SWIVEL/BREAKAWAY ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser had a breached SPM (Smart Pay Module) causing a KSB error.
solution: Replaced the breached Smart Pay Module and re-keyed the debt key. Tested transaction successfully with no errors reported.

problem: Fueling points 4, 5, 7, 8, and 9 were intermittently going offline with error messages on the card reader screens, and all pumps were dispensing at slow flow rates (less than 5 gallons per minute) during regular fuel dispensing.
solution: Technician arrived and performed troubleshooting but was unable to reproduce the problem during the service call. Per the site manager, the slow flow only occurs during heavy usage periods. Technician monitored the site for 30 minutes with no issues observed. Recommend monitoring for recurrence under heavy load conditions.

problem: Dispenser hose 5 and dispenser hose 7 both contained kinks and abrasion damage.
solution: Replaced the whip and hose assemblies on both fueling point 5 and fueling point 7, then purged the lines. Also assisted with reloading the printer paper on fueling point 8 which was not inserted properly.
parts: 2x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S ; 2x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall

problem: Fueling point 12 display freezes when prompting customers to confirm payment method, but only when non-Sam's Club credit cards are used; Sam's Club cards process normally.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found the fueling point fully functional with no issues present. The problem may have been intermittent or related to specific card compatibility or payment tables at the time of the original report.

problem: Fueling point 7 has a black screen.
solution: The technician reset, reloaded, and configured the IX jade board on fueling point 7, then tested it to verify the display was working.

problem: All fuel dispensers at the six fueling points were pumping slowly.
solution: The technician discovered a faulty premium motor was causing the slow dispensing. After checking the breaker and capacitor (both were good), the motor was replaced on the second service visit. The technician monitored the site for 30 minutes and confirmed all dispensers returned to normal operation.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Capacitor Assembly for [400170934] ; 1x FFS-FEP 1-1/2 HP Pump Motor [400276903] ; 1x FFS-FEP 1-1/2 HP Pump Motor [400276903]

problem: Fueling point 10 was offline because the Jade board was not reading the thumb drive.
solution: Replaced the Jade board on fueling point 10, reloaded IDPOS software, and configured the system. Tested to confirm operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU PCB iX Jade Dual Sid

problem: All fueling points could not process prepay or point-of-sale transactions; digital commander units 101 and 102 were no longer connecting; fueling points 7 and 8 showed pay-inside errors.
solution: Technician found the scanner at register 102 was not working and reloaded the register, which resolved the scanner issue and restored connection. Technician also identified that fueling points 5, 7, and 8 have known serial bus (KSB) issues and fueling point 3 needs a jade board, but the customer declined replacement at this time. Technician provided the customer with quotes for parts, labor, and trip charges, as well as a separate quote for two C18 dispenser sets with four dispensers.

problem: Fueling point 12 receipt printer was printing gibberish due to jammed paper; fueling point 11 was stuck on 'one moment please' and would not process payment cards; fueling points 2 and 10 had loose hold-open latch springs on the nozzles causing spills.
solution: Replaced two nozzles on fueling points 2 and 10 and tested them. Power cycled fueling point 11 to fix the card reading issue and tested multiple times. Cleared jammed paper from fueling point 12 receipt printer and verified it now prints correctly. If fueling point 11 payment card issue recurs, the EMV reader will need to be checked.

problem: Fueling point hose was leaking, and the existing automatic tank gauge needed to be replaced.
solution: Technician replaced the whip hose on 1/9/2026. On 1/12/2026, installed a ProGauge Ultimate tank monitor on the 10,000-gallon tank with submersible turbine pump sump sensors, interstitial sensors under the dispenser, and a 101-inch probe. On 1/13/2026, programmed and calibrated the system, tested it, and cleaned up the site.
parts: 1x IRPCO Whip Hose, " x 8" Hardw ; 1x OPW-FMS Sump Sensor-Float Swit ; 1x OPW-FMS Interstitial Sensor-Fl ; 1x OPW-FMS Printer, Thermal (Exte ; 1x OPW-NEW-FMS Float Kit-Gasoline ; 1x OPW-NEW FMS PROBE,RIGID ; 1x OPW FMS ATG MagLinkLX-Ultimate

solution: All control boards were reset, configured, and reloaded. The dispenser controller was reloaded and configured. The system was tested both inside and outside.

problem: Fueling point 6 had two issues: the card reader was continuously flashing green and failing to become operational, and regular-grade fuel transactions were stopping randomly at or before the final prepay amount due to reverse flow detected on the meter exceeding the threshold.
solution: Technician replaced the check valve and flow control valve on the regular-grade meter, secured all fittings, and verified proper dispensing with no leaks. The card reader issue was identified as a continuous reboot cycle that persisted even after downloading the EMV application; however, the corrective action to resolve the card reader malfunction was not completed on this visit and requires further diagnosis.
parts: 1x WAYNE Solenoid Valve Proportio [W2893703-001] ; 1x WAYNE Check Valve and Relief, [WM029220-0001]

problem: All fueling points reported slow flow at less than 5 gallons per minute, and fueling point 4 unihose hold-open latch was not disengaging; premium fuel dispensers had been bagged off.
solution: Technician investigated and found that only premium fuel had been taken offline by site staff who mistakenly thought it was malfunctioning. Technician reopened the premium dispensers and tested all products, confirming all fueling points were operating at 100% capacity.

problem: Nine of ten fueling points were unable to communicate due to an Avalan issue during the equipment installation.
solution: The technician installed a network board as a temporary workaround to allow customers to pump fuel while the Avalan issue was being resolved. The technician recommends installing a new Avalan unit to permanently fix the communication problem.

problem: Fueling point 11 was stuck in a reboot loop and freezing on the Ovation screen at startup, leaving the dispenser out of service.
solution: The technician wiped and reloaded the fueling point 11 software, which restored all functions to normal operation. The dispenser was tested and returned to service.

problem: Fueling point 7 nozzle hold-open latch spring was missing, causing the latch to stick and not release when the tank was full.
solution: Replaced the missing spring on the nozzle hold-open latch and purged the line. All functions returned to normal.

problem: All fueling points in all grades were experiencing slow flow, dispensing at less than 5 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician stopped all pumping operations, had customers wait 15 minutes, then restarted pumping and confirmed normal flow was restored. This suggests an air lock or pressure recovery issue in the system.

problem: Diesel proportional valve was leaking by the meter under the dispenser.
solution: Replaced the proportional valve. The technician verified no leaks were present after the replacement, though pump testing could not be performed due to the station being shut down.

problem: Diesel dispensers were running slow, delivering only about 2 gallons per minute instead of normal flow rate.
solution: Technician removed and inspected the diesel filters on fueling points 1/2 and 3/4, finding large amounts of trash clogging the strainers and contaminating the fuel. Replaced both filters and cleaned the strainers, which restored flow rate to 9-10 gallons per minute. Recommended that the customer have their diesel tank professionally cleaned to prevent recurrence.

problem: Fueling point 1 and 2 were offline due to a failed iGEM board, and fueling point 5 displayed a black and white screen due to an invalid MSI file.
solution: Replaced and paired one smart unit on fueling points 1 and 2, then tested. Loaded the template with branding image from another dispenser to restore fueling point 5 screen display. Technician noted that a return visit is needed to complete repair of the iGEM board and address the MSI file issue.

problem: Fueling point 7 display screen was showing a black screen and not responding.
solution: Replaced the Jade board, loaded the latest iXPay software, completed configuration, and verified operation with a successful test transaction.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Contactless payment reader (EMV) was not functioning on pump 2.
solution: The technician replaced the secure payment module (SPM) and tested it with a debit card to verify proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 was offline and stuck on the Ovation blu screen.
solution: The technician replaced the jade board, reloaded it, and reconfigured it on fueling point 1, then tested the system to confirm operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU PCB iX Jade Dual Sid

problem: Premium grade fuel dispensing at slow flow rate of less than 5 gallons per minute across multiple fueling points, with fueling points 7 and 8 also experiencing meter issues causing transactions to cut short.
solution: Replaced the motor, capacitor, and check valve on the affected fueling point to resolve the slow flow issue. Technician noted that a return trip is needed to address the meter transaction issues on fueling points 7 and 8, and all replaced parts are covered under warranty.
parts: 1x RJ Check Valve Kit ; 1x FFS-FEP Capacitor Assembly for ; 1x FFS-FEP 2 HP Pump Motor Assemb ; 1x WAYNE Solenoid Valve Proportio ; 1x WAYNE Check Valve and Relief,

problem: Fueling points 1, 3, and 6 nozzles were reported as broken with non-functioning auto shutoff, causing overflow and leaking.
solution: Technician tested all fueling points and monitored customer fueling for 45 minutes and found all fueling points operating normally with no issues detected.

problem: The annular space fuel alarm sensor inside the regular submersible turbine pump was not reading and not responding to manual checks.
solution: The technician diagnosed that the sensor inside the regular submersible turbine pump was faulty and non-responsive. The sensor requires replacement, and the technician will need to return to the site to complete the replacement.

problem: Flex hose under the ramp was leaking fuel.
solution: Technician replaced the flex hose and ball valve. Everything is now working fine.
parts: 1x OPW Ball Valve, 2" NPT, Two-Wa [21BV-0200] ; 1x HOSEMASTER Flex Connector, Mal [FSMS360200]

problem: Site is not ready for dispenser installation due to missing infrastructure: split counter design prevents ethernet cable routing, aluminum brackets damaged cable during installation attempt, no suitable conduit exists, and back office lacks shelving and internet connectivity.
solution: Technician attempted to drill holes and route ethernet cable through the wall but was unable to complete installation due to damaged cable and unsuitable conduit options. Installation cannot proceed until the site provides proper cable pathways, installs back office shelving, and establishes internet connectivity.

problem: Fueling points 1 through 12 dispensers required door upgrade from existing units to new AX12 model as part of scheduled installation project.
solution: Technician shut down the site, collected MAC addresses from each new dispenser door before installation, submitted MAC addresses to Walmart technical support for proxy server configuration, then removed all existing dispenser doors and installed new AX12 doors on all fueling points in accordance with Anthem OA PXE Installation Instructions v9.1.

problem: The submersible turbine pump probe at the fuel tank was not communicating with the tank monitor, preventing fuel level checks and automated compliance reports.
solution: After multiple service visits, the technician traced the underground conduit and found two buried junction boxes and broken conduit line. The damaged probe wiring was removed, a pull string was installed from the probe to the main building, and 250 feet of new Beldon B88760 cable was pulled through the conduit and connected from the tank monitor to the probe. The tank monitor was verified to be working properly and compliance reports were restored.
parts: 3x 3M Scotchcast Connector Sealin ; 1x RDM NEW STYLE 101" PROBE TSP-L ; 250x 18-1 SR TNC FEP FOIL SHD

problem: New fueling site reported water in premium fuel detected by state inspector, and the submersible turbine pump controller displayed a locked rotor error.
solution: The technician discovered the POS system was shut down, which caused loss of communication between the dispensers and POS, and reset all control boards and reloaded software to restore dispenser function. A follow-up visit confirmed no water was present in the tank—the inspector had actually found particulates in the fuel; the technician recommended purging the fuel system back to the tank and replacing filters, with a follow-up inspection by the state inspector.

problem: Fueling point 5 customer display was frozen showing all segments (88.88) and not displaying dollar amount or gallon readings, though fuel dispensing worked normally.
solution: Technician swapped the display with the opposite side and confirmed the fault followed the display unit, indicating a faulty display. Replaced the customer display and retested to confirm successful operation. Additionally replaced diesel filters on fueling points 7 and 8, verified flow rate at approximately 8 gallons per minute, and confirmed all dispensers operating correctly.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70065] ; 1x WAYNE Helix, Display, Sales/Vo [WM041368-0002]

problem: During pump and point-of-sale installation, the system experienced payment processing errors due to incorrect server configuration, and the fuel price display channel was causing the fuel driver to lock up and freeze.
solution: The technician corrected the server settings, wiped and reloaded the system, then reprogrammed it manually. After disabling the problematic fuel price display channel, all functions resumed normal operation. The technician tested all pumps and verified that tap, card insertion, and contactless functions were working correctly.

problem: The ATG tank monitor printer was not working because the Integra device had lost its programming for printing inventory, delivery, and tank test reports.
solution: Reprogrammed the Integra to print out each morning and tested all functions; everything is now working at 100%.

problem: Fueling points 8 and 12 were out of service due to recurring fuel overrun issues at the dispenser.
solution: The technician replaced the breakaway swivel combo on fueling points 5, 6, and 12, and replaced nozzles on fueling points 8, 9, and 10 due to overflows. The swivel breakaway combos were leaking and causing the overrun; all replaced nozzles are under warranty.
parts: 3x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 3x OPW 3/4" SWIVEL/BREAKAWAY ; 1x OPW 3/4" SWIVEL/BREAKAWAY

problem: Auto shut-off nozzles failed on fueling points 5, 6, and 10, causing leaks and spills; fueling points 1 and 12 had drive-off pump issues.
solution: Replaced nozzles on fueling points 5 and 10; confirmed fueling point 6 had no problem. Reloaded and reconfigured the fueling point controller (IGem) on fueling points 5 and 6. Technician recommended returning with two breakaway assemblies to repair fueling points 1 and 12.
parts: 2x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 3x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 3x OPW 3/4" SWIVEL/BREAKAWAY

problem: Fueling point 7 was reported to be stuck on a 'please wait a moment' screen, but no problem was found on that unit; however, the card reader on fueling point 5 was not reading cards.
solution: The card reader on fueling point 5 was replaced and tested. No corrective action was needed on fueling point 7.

problem: Fueling point 9 dispenser printer was not printing receipts and displaying a red light despite attempts to reload paper and test print.
solution: Technician found a loose connection on the back of the printer that prevented communication with the Jay board. Technician cycled power to the printer, reinstalled cables correctly, and verified all printer functions returned to normal operation. Technician also repaired the printer lock assembly which was damaged.

problem: The automated tank gauge (ATG) system was offline and unable to poll tank gauges.
solution: The technician reseated the CAT5 communication cable and power-cycled the ATG system. Technical support confirmed the ATG was back online and reporting data accurately before the technician left the site.

problem: Customer needed to change the time settings on the ATG tank monitor and configure automatic report printing for the SLD report.
solution: Technician updated the date/time settings on the OPW Integra 100 tank monitor and configured the SLD report to print automatically on a weekly schedule instead of manual printing.

problem: Multiple dispensers had issues: two sales units needed replacement on fueling points 3/4 and 7/8, premium fuel was not dispensing on 7/8 due to a closed sphere valve, and fueling point 6 card reader was not connected.
solution: Replaced two sales units and paired them on fueling points 3/4 and 7/8. Opened the premium sphere valve on fueling point 7/8 to restore premium fuel dispensing. For fueling point 6, the customer approved a POS C18 upgrade to resolve the card reader connection issue.

problem: The premium tank probe was not reporting correct readings because rust in the submersible turbine pump check valve was holding it open, preventing proper system pressure.
solution: The technician removed rust and debris from the check valve and submersible turbine pump using magnets and rags, then cleaned and reinstalled the check valve. The system was pressurized to verify integrity, a 500-gallon purge was performed to rinse out remaining rust, and all components were reinstalled and tested successfully.

problem: Pump 4 displayed a KSB error and required replacement of the SPM (sales/service processor module).
solution: Technician replaced the SPM on fueling point 4 and tested the equipment to confirm proper operation.

problem: Dispenser number 5 proportional valve was faulty on the regular product, causing the display to scroll continuously and preventing calibration checks.
solution: Replaced the solenoid valve and verified that calibration returned to within tolerance. The dealer will have the state return for a final calibration verification.

problem: The nozzle boot on fueling point 9 was damaged or worn and needed replacement.
solution: Replaced the nozzle boot on fueling point 9. All functions tested normal after replacement.

problem: The non-ethanol fuel reading and report were not showing on the dispenser for premium and regular 87 fueling points.
solution: Installed new probes for both the premium and regular 87 fueling points, then programmed them and tested all readings to confirm they were reading correctly at 100%.

problem: The dispenser system was completely down with a blank outside computer antenna screen, and the breaker was found to be faulty.
solution: Technician identified that the breaker needs to be replaced and recommended that the dealer hire a licensed electrician to perform the replacement.

problem: Fueling points 6 and 7 EMV card readers were not reading cards reliably, with fueling point 7 showing intermittent card transaction acceptance.
solution: The technician tested both card readers, confirmed intermittent operation on fueling point 7, and replaced that card reader. Both fueling points were then returned online and tested with multiple successful transactions. Fueling point 7 is now operating normally, and fueling point 6 was verified to be reading cards successfully with no issues.

problem: Fueling point 1 dispenser was not accepting cards at the pump due to a card reader that was not responding.
solution: The technician replaced the card reader and discovered a bad connection port on the system processor module (SPM), which prevented activation. On a return visit, the SPM was replaced and software was uploaded to restore card acceptance service on fueling point 1. A valve repair on unit 5 remains pending and requires a separate return visit.

problem: The keypad on the fueling point 4 pinpad was malfunctioning, with half the keys not working or registering double presses.
solution: Replaced the pinpad and injected debit encryption. Tested the fueling point and confirmed all functions were normal.

problem: Fueling point 6 was offline and displaying an error message on the display screen.
solution: The technician performed an iXReset and debug on the Jade board, then reloaded and configured the board. After allowing the fueling point to fully load, a test transaction was performed successfully, and the fueling point was returned to operational status.

problem: Fueling point 7/8 customer display was black with no video output.
solution: Technician performed iXPay debug and reload on the Jade board. Fueling point 7/8 display is now operating normally and transactions are completing successfully.

problem: Fueling point 3 was stuck in a continuous reboot loop at the Ovation startup screen.
solution: The technician performed a debug/iXReset on the Jade board, reconfigured it, and rebooted the dispenser. They allowed fueling point 3 to fully load from the register and completed a test transaction. Additionally, they replaced the keypad due to a keypad security breach error and applied Shell debit encryption.
Not stated
parts: 1x M400 PIN PAD VERIFONE SYSTEMS [M445-403-01-WWA-5] ; 1x VERIFONE Multiport Cable Assy, [MSC445-011-00-A] ; 1x VERIFONE PS+Ferrite, 120VAC/12 [PWR445-001-01-A] ; 1x VERIFONE 367-5249-A LOW CONTO [STA000-004-01-A]

problem: Fueling point 12 unihose nozzle came loose from the hose, causing a leak, and the fueling point was shut down.
solution: The technician arrived on site and confirmed with the fuel attendant that the fueling point 1 breakaway had been repaired. The attendant confirmed there have been no leaks since the repair and fueling point 1 is currently operating normally.

problem: Multiple dispensers were experiencing intermittent automatic shutoff, particularly during low ambient temperature conditions, despite normal fuel flow rates.
solution: Technician inspected the affected dispensers and tested flow rates using regular unleaded fuel, measuring approximately 8 gallons per minute. The technician monitored operation on site for one hour and observed no auto shutoff issues during this period, suggesting the problem may be temperature-dependent or intermittent in nature.

problem: Fueling point 4 was displaying a fatal system error message and had a history of repeatedly going offline.
solution: Not stated
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Tank 2 submersible turbine pump probe is not reading and regular grade fuel is not dispensing.
solution: Not stated
parts: 2x OPW Micro-DC Plug 3 Pole 6ft C [10-1185] ; 2x 3M Scotchcast Connector Sealin [3570G-N] ; 1x FFS-FEP 3/4 HP Pump Motor Asse [400276902]

solution: Not stated

problem: The Veeder Root printer was not printing fuel delivery receipts showing tank levels.
solution: The technician diagnosed the issue and determined the printer was faulty and not communicating with the CPU. On a return trip, the technician replaced the printer with a new unit that is now functioning properly.

problem: Regular unleaded, premium unleaded, and diesel dispenser inner lids contained cracks and chips throughout, and multiple underground dispenser controller sumps contained trace amounts of liquid below the pump boots.
solution: Technician cleaned approximately 1 gallon of water from the sumps and documented the lid cracks with photos. Per supervisor review, the cracked lids do not require replacement unless water intrusion becomes an ongoing issue.

problem: Fueling point 3/4 customer display was flashing 'Closed' and the dispenser was offline.
solution: Entered the door security one-shot code to clear the Closed error condition. Performed a test transaction to verify the fueling point is operating properly.

problem: The ATG tank monitor had two configuration issues: it did not allow manual printing of sensor status reports for release detection, and the submersible turbine pump sump sensors were incorrectly labeled as dispenser sensors.
solution: Not stated

problem: The electrical breaker was tripping when starting the dispenser.
solution: The technician checked all voltages to the dispenser, tested both motors with a fuel card (both at 100%), verified the internal power supply was within tolerance, and found no fault. They advised the dealer to monitor the equipment and report any further issues.

problem: All fueling points at multiple grades were dispensing very slowly (penny by penny flow rate), with premium grade showing pressure decay from 30+ PSI to 10 PSI during high-flow operation, caused by a compromised main line leak detector (MLLD) at the bottom connection of the copper-lined tubing on the premium submersible turbine pump sump.
solution: Technician performed diagnostics on fueling points 5/6 and 11/12 in standalone mode using calibrated pressure gauges, identified an active leak at the MLLD copper-lined tubing connection on the premium submersible turbine pump, and determined the MLLD was compromised. The MLLD should be replaced to restore proper fuel flow and system pressure.
parts: 3x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 1x VAPORLESS Leak Detector

problem: Fueling point 2 unihose nozzle auto shutoff was not engaging because the latch spring was missing.
solution: Replaced the nozzle and purged air from the system. Tested the nozzle and confirmed all functions operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 5 auto shut-off nozzle was not engaging, causing fuel leaks; fueling point 1 hose was run over with the nozzle disconnected at the breakaway; fueling point 7 occasionally gets stuck on 'wait one moment' after card insertion.
solution: Technician tested the fueling point 5 nozzle and confirmed auto shut-off is now engaging properly. Inspected fueling point 1 hose, breakaway, and nozzle and found them in good condition. All three fueling points were returned to service and technician confirmed customers were using fueling points 5 and 1. No parts were replaced.

problem: Fueling point 8 unleaded nozzle auto shut-off was not engaging, causing spills.
solution: Technician tested the auto shut-off mechanism and confirmed it engages properly. The issue was caused by customers not inserting the nozzle completely into their vehicle's fill hole, which prevents the auto shut-off from triggering. Fueling point 8 unleaded is now back in service.

problem: Pump 2 EMV reader displayed an error on screen.
solution: The Secure PIN Module (SPM) was replaced and injected with Shell debit key. The system was tested with a debit card to verify proper operation.

problem: Multiple dispensers (pump #2, #3, #6, and #8) displayed blank screens, preventing customers from using the equipment.
solution: Troubleshooted the dispensers. Technician determined that further service is needed and will return with Dover Fueling Solutions equipment for fueling points #3, #4, #7, and #8.

problem: Fueling point 5 EMV payment terminal was displaying a KSB error and required replacement.
solution: The technician replaced the SPM (secure payment module) on fueling point 5 and tested the replacement unit with a debit card to confirm proper operation.

problem: Premium fuel was not dispensing on any fueling points, and regular fuel was also not dispensing; the issue was ongoing.
solution: The technician tested the premium grade fuel system, found no pressure, and traced the problem to a failed submersible turbine pump motor at the tank pad. After verifying voltage was present at the breaker, the technician removed and replaced the submersible turbine pump motor along with the 40-micron capacitor, cleaned the motor assembly, and reinstalled it.
parts: 1x RJ Capacitor Kit, 40 M ; 1x FFS-FEP 2 HP Variable Speed Pu ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 4 main display was frozen on 'One Moment To Check' and the dispenser would not reboot, leaving the pump out of service.
solution: Reloaded both the SCD and Jade board, reconfigured the Jade board, and rebooted the dispenser. After allowing fueling point 4 to fully load, a test transaction was performed successfully and the dispenser returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling points 5 and 12 receipt printers were not printing after transactions completed.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found paper jams in both printers. Cleared the jams and tested both printers; all functions returned to normal operation.

problem: The card reader printer on fueling point 12 was not feeding paper due to a bent printer door latch lever that prevented the door from closing securely.
solution: The technician straightened the bent latch lever and properly secured the printer door. The error was cleared and the printer was verified to be functioning correctly and printing receipts.

problem: Dispenser door panels and bottom panels were installed facing the wrong direction, with the swoop design reversed.
solution: Reversed all side panels and bottom door panels to face the correct direction. Imaging will need to return and swap out the valances on all five dispensers to match the correct swoop orientation.

problem: Fueling point 4 was offline due to full memory in the dispenser control receiver, and multiple fueling points had drive-off damage to breakaway assemblies and a submersible turbine pump failure on fueling point 3.
solution: Technician replaced the breakaway, hose, swivel, and nozzle on fueling point 4, then purged and reloaded the IXJade software to bring the dispenser control receiver back online. On fueling point 2, technician reattached the breakaway. On fueling point 3, technician replaced the failed submersible turbine pump and reloaded the IXJade to confirm it was up to date. All systems were tested and confirmed operational.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x ; 1x CENTRAL Breakaway, ", Catlow ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK

problem: Fueling point 5 (unleaded) and fueling point 8 (diesel) were leaking from the nozzles and taken out of service.
solution: Technician tested both fueling points and confirmed the auto-shutoff mechanisms were functional. The leaking was likely due to slow fuel flow from extremely cold weather conditions or customer error, as auto-shutoff valves require adequate pressure to operate properly.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 7 autoshutoff was not engaging, causing fuel spillage. The site reported this issue was occurring on all fueling points and suspected it might be related to cold weather.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found the pumps in service and functioning normally. The issue was determined to be possibly user error rather than equipment malfunction.

problem: Fueling point 4 displayed 'pay at pump currently unavailable' and could not accept prepay allocations from the inside terminal; the fueling point was out of service.
solution: The technician found a communication failure between the IXJade controller and the card reader. They wiped and reloaded the IXJade software, which resolved the issue. The technician tested all functions and confirmed normal operation; they also powered off and rebooted fueling point 6 after the customer had crashed its card reader, and the system returned to normal.

problem: The regular unleaded (RUL) and premium unleaded (PUL) submersible turbine pump sumps each contained pockets of liquid below the boot seals.
solution: The technician arrived on site and removed 4 gallons of water from the sumps.

problem: Fueling point #9 and #10 nozzles were leaking, and fueling point #2 breakaway was leaking; additionally, diesel hoses on fueling points #10, #9, #2, and #1 were leaking from the top connections.
solution: Replaced the broken breakaways on dispensers #2 and #10 with new units. Cleaned and tightened all diesel hose connections at the top of fueling points #10, #9, #2, and #1. Tested the entire system and confirmed all equipment is working properly.

problem: Fueling point 3 was offline due to drive-offs on multiple pumps, and fueling point 4 had a full memory error caused by missing breakaway and hose components.
solution: Technician reattached the breakaway on fueling point 2 and verified no further issues. On fueling point 4, technician installed a new breakaway, hose, swivel, and nozzle, then purged the system.

problem: All fueling points at the newly opened store were showing incorrect fuel grades at each dispenser due to improperly configured hose mapping in the system.
solution: Technician reconfigured the hose mapping on the commander to assign the correct fuel grades (Regular, Mid-grade, Premium, and Diesel) to their proper hoses on all dispensers, then resent the DCR (display controller reset) configuration to all pumps. Fueling point 7 required the DCR to be sent twice to synchronize properly.

problem: Fueling point 3 nozzle auto shut-off was not engaging at the end of fill-ups, causing customer vehicle tanks to overfill.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 3 and tested it to confirm proper operation. While on-site, technician also checked fueling point 7 which the customer reported had the same issue, verified it was working properly on both regular and premium grades, and confirmed both fueling points and the station returned to full operational status.

problem: Dispenser not accepting credit card transactions at inside and outside fueling points.
solution: Technician tested transactions at both inside and outside dispensers and checked network online status; all systems tested good and confirmed operational.

problem: Fuel pumps 2, 3, and 5 were declining all card transactions at the EMV reader.
solution: Technician reset all three fueling points and cycled power to the commander system to refresh it. All card reader issues were resolved.

problem: All fueling point pumps were offline and not working.
solution: Technician found that the regular motor and submersible turbine pump start capacitor were locked up. Replaced the start capacitor and motor with a 1.5 horsepower motor, then tested all pumps successfully.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP 1-1/2 HP Pump Motor [400276903] ; 1x AMERICAN CONTAINMENT CAPACITOR [4182519]
