problem: Premium grade selection was not working on fueling point 2, card transactions were not processing on fueling points 1 and 2, and the road sign was displaying incorrect premium fuel price.
solution: Technician power cycled fueling point 2 to restore grade select functionality, then power cycled again to restore card transaction processing on both fueling points. For the road sign price display issue, the vendor pushed out price updates and then technician power cycled the sign breaker to display the correct premium fuel price.

problem: Three fueling points were out of service: fueling point 11 displayed 'Card Reader Unavailable' and would not clear with reboot, fueling point 1 had the Ovation screen stuck and would not reboot, and fueling point 6 displayed 'SDC Waiting' and would not resolve with reboot.
solution: Technician arrived on site and corrected fueling point 6 by wiping and reloading the EMV reader unit, which tested normal afterward. Fueling point 11 showed no issue during the visit and was likely user error. Fueling point 1 had the Ovation IX screen stuck; wiping did not resolve it, so further action was taken (details not stated).
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Pump 11 was unable to dispense diesel exhaust fluid (DEF) because the nozzle had no pressure and would not open.
solution: The technician removed the nozzle and confirmed that DEF pumped normally without it, indicating the nozzle was faulty. A new nozzle was installed and tested to verify full functionality. The customer was charged for the service since the nozzle was not a Wayne-branded part.

problem: Fueling point 6 was unable to read card payments because the card reader was not clasping cards properly.
solution: Technician arrived on site and tested the card reader in diagnostics mode, finding it functional. After testing with actual payment transactions and allowing customer use, no issues were found. The card reader operated normally.

problem: Fueling point 1 automatic shut-off nozzle was not engaging, causing fuel spills and requiring the pump to be taken out of service.
solution: Technician tested the nozzle, confirmed it was unable to stop when triggered, and replaced it with a new nozzle. After replacement, the shut-off function was verified to be working properly.

problem: Fueling point 6 printer door lock was stuck in the open position with a broken key, preventing receipt printing. Additionally, fueling point 1 was out of service due to a leak caused by incorrect sealant applied to the swivel and nozzle.
solution: Technician used a modified key to return the fueling point 6 printer lock to the secured position. On fueling point 1, technician removed the swivel and nozzle components, applied the correct pipe sealant, reinstalled the parts, and performed a pump test to verify no leaks remained.

problem: Fueling point 5 display screen was fuzzy and difficult to read, experiencing intermittent shaking and shutting off despite reset attempts, leaving the fueling point out of service.
solution: Technician performed diagnostics and found the VGA display was failing with a white screen and vertical lines. The display was replaced with a verified working spare unit, all cabling was secured, and power was restored. Fueling point 5 was confirmed to be online and functioning normally.

problem: The dispenser's submersible turbine pump was not working properly, and the card reader had also failed.
solution: Replaced the submersible turbine pump with a new unit and tested it to verify proper operation. Replaced the card reader with a new unit and tested it to verify proper operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se [W2893895-002] ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700]

problem: All four drop tubes on the dispenser were worn out or damaged and needed replacement.
solution: Replaced all four drop tubes. One drop tube required an additional riser seal because the old tube had to be cut off for removal. Customer to be billed for parts and labor.
parts: 1x EMCO Riser Seal, Stainless Ste ; 4x EMCO Overfill Prevention Valve

problem: Power was not reaching the dispenser even though it was connected, with the emergency stop breaker found in the off position.
solution: The technician completed the installation of two new C18 registers to replace the old Topaz registers, organized and zip-tied the wiring neatly, and tested the system to confirm it was operational. The emergency stop breaker should be turned on to restore power to the dispensers.

problem: Fueling point 9 autoshut was engaging intermittently during fueling, causing the dispenser to be taken out of service.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 9. After replacement, the dispenser was returned to service and no further autoshut issues were observed.

problem: Fueling point 6 display screen was showing white with no image.
solution: Technician power cycled the Anthem board directly, and the unit booted up normally. System was tested with a debit transaction and appears to be functioning normally.

problem: The Inconvenience Store 750 printer slide bar is stuck and will not operate, preventing receipt printing, and the display screen is black.
solution: Technician determined the printer mechanism is defective and cannot be repaired. Technician coordinated with the dealer and Apec to replace the entire unit with a new EVO 200 system after January 1st; dealer will provide detailed pricing for labor and parts.

problem: Multiple fueling point issues: FP 4 satellite refusing to send signal to master dispenser, FP 4 handle switch failure, FP 4 nozzle boot deterioration, FP 2 relay board failure preventing motor startup, FP 2 slow flow, and FP 3 filter leaking.
solution: Replaced handle switch and nozzle boot on FP 4 satellite, replaced relay board on FP 2, and replaced filters on FP 2 and FP 3 to resolve flow and leak issues. Technician noted that if FP 4 signal issue persists after these repairs, the IGEM or pipe assembly may require replacement and return visit.
parts: 2x CIMTEK Filter, 800 Series 10 [70063] ; 7x CIMTEK Filter, 800 Series 10 [70063] ; 1x WYN iGEM CPU Board [WM001908-0013-R]

problem: Two newly installed dispensers at fueling points 7 and 8 have multiple payment processing failures: fueling point 8 tap-to-pay is not accepting payments, and fueling point 7 rejects inserted cards immediately and displays error messages instead of processing transactions; additionally, premium grade fuel dispensed extremely slowly on fueling point 8.
solution: Technician replaced leaking whip hoses and 8-foot hoses on both dispensers, reloaded Jade boards with latest Shell-approved iXPay software, configured iGem and upgraded firmware on tap-to-pay devices, re-enabled fueling point 7 and 8 settings at the Commander, initialized fuel and DCRs, and rebooted Commander and Edge Pro systems. However, payment processing issues on both fueling points and slow premium fuel dispensing on fueling point 8 remain unresolved after multiple calls to Shell and Verifone support; a return visit is required for further troubleshooting.

problem: Commander interface base unit was not functioning properly, and one register touch screen was not responding.
solution: Replaced the defective Commander interface base unit with a replacement unit and re-paired all point-of-sale devices. Tested transactions both inside and outside the system to verify proper operation. Identified keyboard issues on some security pin modules but customer declined replacement. The site was re-registered in the system.
parts: 1x 8848                       RDM Commander 16 Ruby CI Base ; 1x VERIFONE Battery Pack Ruby2 [BPK169-001-01-A]

problem: Fueling point 1 was going offline repeatedly after a previous technician reset the SCD and Jade board; fueling point 4 was unable to conduct transactions; fueling point 7 printer was not working.
solution: Reloaded and reconfigured the Jade board on fueling point 1 and tested it. Reconfigured the printer on fueling point 7 and brought it back online. Fueling point 4 requires a hose set replacement (hose, nozzle, and breakaway) and will need a return visit to complete.

problem: Fuel alarm triggered at the unleaded tank 2 submersible turbine pump sump due to water accumulation in the sump sensor.
solution: Technician removed 115 gallons of water from the sump. Recommend verifying the alarm condition has been resolved by calling 1-800-997-7725, option 1 then option 2.

problem: EMV PIN entry device would not process transactions and required a full software update.
solution: Technician contacted Citgo support to update dispenser tables, then downloaded the updated EMV PIN entry device tables and closed the business day to clear the error state. After reopening, technician tested both indoor and outdoor fueling points and confirmed all systems were operating normally.

problem: Multiple fueling points (dispensers 3/4, 7/8, and 11/12) contained trace amounts of water accumulated in the submersible turbine pump sumps below the boots.
solution: Technician arrived on site and removed approximately 5 gallons of water from the affected pump sumps.

problem: Fueling point 5 card reader was declining all cards of all types, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Technician wiped and reloaded the IXJade system software to resolve the issue.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: One side of the dispenser was not dispensing fuel because the mechanical computer on that side had failed.
solution: Technician replaced the mechanical computer (model G6201D, serial 93417) on the gasoline dispenser and replaced the leak detector on the diesel submersible turbine pump tank. After testing, both sides dispensed fuel normally at 8 to 12 gallons per minute.
parts: 1x V/R NON-COMPUTER FOR WAYNE [724101] ; 1x 99 LD2200               VAPORLESS Leak Detector

problem: Site survey needed for dispenser replacement at Murphy USA location.
solution: Technician performed dispenser swap survey on December 10, 2025, and completed documentation in Appenate and Oracle systems per Murphy USA Dispenser Survey Project specifications.

problem: Dispenser lost network connectivity after customer changed their internet router and the MTI box needed to be synced to the new network.
solution: Located the new AT&T router and contacted My Tank Info support, who tunneled into a laptop to update the MTI box software and IP address. After reconfiguration, confirmed remote access to the Integra 100 dispenser was restored.

problem: Fueling point 8 was showing out of service and stuck on SCD ready mode; power cycling did not resolve the issue. Fueling point 6 was not reading payment cards.
solution: Technician determined that the SCD (secure card reader) for fueling point 8 is defective and requires replacement, and replaced the card reader on fueling point 6. A return visit is needed to install the replacement SCD for fueling point 8.

problem: Fueling point 12 uni-hose was leaking near the handle at the nozzle swivel during use, and fueling point 5 display was stuck showing 'SPM Operational' message.
solution: Replaced the nozzle and breakaway assembly on fueling point 12 and verified the pump dispenses without leaks. Reloaded the jade board with IDPOS software on fueling point 5 to resolve the stuck reboot cycle. Recommend monitoring fueling point 12 closely as this issue had reoccurred from a previous repair.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x CENTRAL Breakaway, ", Catlow

problem: Fueling point 2/11 card readers were reported as showing unavailable.
solution: Technician inspected fueling point 2/11 and found no issues; the card readers are functioning properly and the fueling points are online and processing customer transactions normally. No corrective action was needed.

problem: Fueling point 3 unihose auto shutoff was not engaging, causing small spills.
solution: Technician reloaded the Jade board and the SCD (Scan & Go Control Device), then reconfigured the Jade board and allowed it to fully load. A test transaction was run to verify operation and fueling point 8 was confirmed online and operational. Note: Scan & Go functionality could not be fully tested due to technician access limitations, but the auto shutoff issue was resolved.

problem: The ATG tank monitor was displaying an active warning alarm indicating an upgrade was available, even though the unit had already been upgraded to software version 10.P.233.58.
solution: The technician verified the current software version on site and attempted to resolve the issue by power cycling the unit. When the warning alarm persisted, the technician contacted the equipment coordinator who will arrange with the Veeder-Root support team to clear the warning alarm remotely.

problem: Fueling point 1 had a hole in the unihose with exposed wires.
solution: The technician replaced the hose on fueling point 1 and tested the pump.
Removed old floats and installed new floats on the replacement probe, then tested to confirm proper operation.

problem: All fuel grades were dispensing very slowly, one penny at a time.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found all pumps running at normal flow rates with no issues present during the visit. No corrective action was required.

problem: Keypad board error on fueling point 1 dispenser.
solution: Replaced the keypad board on fueling point 1 and tested the system.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 3 were not reading payment cards.
solution: Technician determined that both Satellite Payment Modules were not alphanumeric and were programmed improperly for the NCR system. The IXJade programming on fueling point 4 also requires attention. For fueling points 3 and 4, the issue appears to be either the IGEM or pipe assembly for the satellite, as it refuses to send a signal to the master even after bootstrapping the IGEM; these components may need replacement or further diagnosis.

problem: All unleaded fuel filters needed to be replaced.
solution: Technician arrived on site and replaced all unleaded filters on every fueling point pump.

problem: Fueling point 1 hose was damaged and crushed from being run over, and fueling point 3 unihose retractor spring snapped causing the hose to hang down.
solution: Replaced the nozzle, swivel, and hose on fueling point 1. Repaired the draw string on fueling point 3 retractor. Both fueling points returned to service.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: Fueling point 1 unihose nozzle was leaking during use, and fueling point 3 had a damaged draw string.
solution: Replaced the nozzle swivel and hose on fueling point 1, and replaced the draw string on fueling point 3. Purged and tested fueling point 1 to confirm the leak was resolved.

problem: Fueling point 1 main screen displayed a fatal error message and the dispenser was out of service.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 1 was offline and not responding.
solution: The technician rebooted the dispenser and it came back online. All systems were tested and found to be operating normally.
Technician reset the SCD card on the J board for dispenser 1 and tested it successfully. Technician will monitor dispenser 1 for any further issues.

problem: Fueling point 1 had a broken nozzle hold-open latch spring causing spills and was out of service; fueling point 2 had a broken retractor cable but was still in service.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 1. Technician inspected fueling point 2 and salvaged the remaining nylon cable after confirming all systems appeared normal.

problem: The dispenser control board (J board) was programmed incorrectly, causing the soft keys for yes/no acknowledgment to not work, and the dispenser would not communicate with the point-of-sale system or recognize Speedy Rewards.
solution: Reprogrammed the J board and reloaded its firmware, then rebooted the NCR point-of-sale system at the dispenser. All four high-speed diesel receipt printers were also found to be out of paper for an extended period; paper was installed and the printers were rebooted to recognize the system again. All equipment is now functioning at 100%.

problem: Tank monitor was showing a high warning level after testing, likely due to cold temperature conditions affecting the tank gauge reading.
solution: Reprogrammed the tank test sensitivity from moderate to extreme to account for cold temperature effects on the tank probe. Technician also advised the dealer on potential future issues with their fueling station setup.

problem: Fueling point 8 card reader was not reading any cards, and multiple keypad buttons were unresponsive.
solution: Technician replaced the card reader and keypad, then performed test transactions to confirm fueling point 8 was operating normally.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK

solution: EMV and ANTX systems were upgraded on three simulators. No point-of-sale system was on site.

problem: Fueling points 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 were not printing receipts due to paper jams in the printers.
solution: Technician arrived on site and cleared the paper jams from the printers at fueling points 2, 3, 4, and 6, then re-fed paper into each printer. All printers are now working and printing receipts correctly. No parts were replaced.

For fueling point 2, reloaded and configured the jade board, then tested it. For fueling point 8, injected and replaced one SPM (sales processor module). For fueling point 12, reloaded and configured the jade board, then tested it.

problem: Fueling point 6 card reader was declining debit cards and only accepting credit cards, and Scan and Go transactions were not being accepted.
solution: Technician investigated the card reader and verified debit encryption using the RKI application, confirming that the correct debit key (892638-006-102) was already properly injected. The root cause and final resolution are not stated in the notes.
parts: 1x WAYNE Cable, USB, A Male to B

problem: Fueling points 1, 7, and 8 had blank displays and were offline; fueling point 1 failed to boot and fueling points 7 and 8 had no power despite the breaker being on.
solution: Power cycled fueling point 1 and verified the jumper setting on the IXJade module, which restored normal operation. For fueling points 7 and 8, opened the junction box and found the neutral connection was loose; adjusted and tested the connection to restore power and normal function to both pumps.

Installed dual C18/Commander system with latest Buypass Valero software, Mako box and network switch; verified network connectivity and debit/credit testing; confirmed ValeroPay+ and POP functionality for Valero cards at $0.10 initial value; installed indoor wireless AvaLAN system and established communication with two AX12 dispensers running Media DX Promote; documented configuration through FastField photos. Customer declined all reporting functions (End of Day, Month, Year, and Payroll).

problem: Fueling point 12 display screen was stuck on the Ovation screen and would not respond or return to normal operation.
solution: The technician rebooted the Jade controller and the entire dispenser multiple times without success, then attempted to force reload the software using thumb drives. After these troubleshooting steps failed, the technician replaced the Jade board on both fueling point 8 and fueling point 12, reloaded proper software, verified communication with the site contact, and tested all operations to confirm proper functionality.
parts: 2x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fueling point 1 door panel would not lock and kept opening.
solution: Technician adjusted the upper electronic door lock to secure it properly and tested the door. The lock now functions correctly and all other equipment tested at 100%.

problem: Tank 3 diesel tank probe was not reporting inventory or float movement, causing a probe-out alarm that repeatedly reactivated over several days.
solution: Technician arrived on site and replaced the diesel tank probe. All alarms were reset on the Veeder-Root monitor and have cleared.

problem: Fueling point 8 auto shutoff nozzle was not engaging when customers released the pump handle, causing fuel to continue dispensing and spill.
solution: Technician arrived on site and tested the fueling point; no issues or spills were observed during the visit. The problem was likely user error in operating the nozzle. Trip charge was waived.

problem: Fueling point 6 displayed 'USB disconnected' error with date and time, and fueling point 10 displayed 'Card reader unavailable' error and would not accept any form of payment; reboots did not resolve the issue.
solution: Reloaded the Jade board on fueling point 6 and reconfigured it, then tested to verify the fix.

problem: Fueling point 6 was stuck in a reboot loop and out of service; fueling point 12 uni-hose nozzle was intermittently leaking near the bolt at the end of the nozzle handle.
solution: Reloaded and reconfigured the jade board in fueling point 6 and tested it. Tightened the hose connection at the nozzle on fueling point 12 and tested to confirm no leaks.

problem: Fueling point 9 printer had a paper jam and the control arm was not fully seated, causing the red error light and preventing paper from feeding.
solution: Technician cleared the paper jam and fully seated the control arm. All functions tested and confirmed working at 100%.

problem: Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) was stuck in a reboot loop with all tank sensors in alarm state.
solution: Technician rebooted the ATG and contacted Fuel Operations to remotely clear all sensor alarms. All functions returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 has a snapped uni-hose retractor cable and is out of service. Fueling point 6 is stuck in a reboot loop on the Ovation IX screen.
solution: For fueling point 6, the technician replaced the IXJade module, loaded and programmed it, then waited for network connectivity to be established. All functions tested normal after completion. For fueling point 1, not stated.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

solution: Not stated
parts: 7x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se [W2893895-002]

problem: Fueling point 7 regular unleaded grade would not select and automatically defaulted to premium grade when the nozzle was lifted.
solution: Powered down the dispenser and reseated all cables on the PPU board, then restored power and retested. All grades now select and dispense correctly.

problem: Receipt printer on fueling points 11, 12, and 13 was not printing, and card readers were clamping.
solution: Technician tested all card readers on fueling points 11, 12, and 13 with the customer and found no issues. Technician cleared a paper jam from the printer. All equipment is now functioning properly.

problem: Fueling point 5 had a non-functional PPU display and fueling point 6 had no flow.
solution: Technician troubleshot both fueling points by placing the dispenser into standalone mode and verified both could dispense all grades successfully. After testing confirmed no faults, technician contacted Wayne Level 2 support (reference 483705) to report findings. No issues were identified during testing; consider contacting Wayne support if problems recur.

problem: Fueling point 7 unleaded stopped dispensing after a few dollars and was taken out of service.
solution: The technician arrived and ran a test transaction on the fueling point, which completed successfully. Upon inspection, a hole was found in the 4 foot whip hose on fueling point 3 causing a leak. The technician replaced the damaged hose with a new 4 foot whip hose, secured all fittings, and performed a test dispense to confirm no leaks remained.

problem: Veeder Root 4 tank monitor needed installation at the site.
solution: Installed the tank monitor, connected probes and sensor leads, reconfigured wiring, calibrated all tanks, and verified all readings were correct.

problem: Unleaded filters on fueling points 1, 2, 7, 8, 15, and 16 needed replacement.
solution: Technician replaced the unleaded filters on all six fueling points (1, 2, 7, 8, 15, and 16) and tested all pumps on both sides to confirm proper dispensing operation.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were offline and customers could not pay at the pump, although prepay was working.
solution: The technician replaced the j board and discovered the wireless box was the root cause. They swapped the wireless box from fueling points 5/6 to 7/8, which restored 7/8 to service. They replaced one SU (secure unit) on fueling point 7/8, paired it, and tested a transaction successfully. A return visit is needed to replace the wireless box permanently to resolve the issue with fueling points 5/6 being down.

problem: Fueling point 6 was displaying a blank/black screen and a 'Please Pay Inside' error message, making the dispenser inoperable.
solution: The technician replaced the unresponsive Jade board, installed the latest iXPay software, completed configuration, and reloaded the DCR and EMV files from the register. After power-cycling the dispenser and performing successful test transactions on both sides, fueling point 6 was restored to full operating condition.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Pump 2 displayed a KSB error on the dispenser.
solution: Not stated. Technician determined that the door needs to be replaced and advised that Summit Petroleum will provide authorization to proceed.

problem: Fueling point 3 dispenser displayed a black screen and was not responsive to reset attempts.
solution: Technician replaced the unresponsive Jade board and performed a full reload and configuration. After reboot, a breach condition was detected, so the SPM keypad was also replaced and debit key encryption was applied. The dispenser was returned to operational mode and a test transaction confirmed successful operation. Note: Jade board replacement is covered under APEC warranty.
parts: 1x WAYNE Keypad Assembly, SPM2, U ; 0x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fueling point 8 EMV card reader was not accepting chip card transactions, only magstripe transactions.
solution: Replaced the card reader on fueling point 8 and performed test transactions to confirm proper operation. The fueling point is now accepting both magstripe and EMV chip transactions.

problem: Unleaded tank 2 was showing repeat stick levels because the float was stuck and the tank probe was indicating high level and high overfill limit.
solution: The technician removed the tank 2 probe from the tank, cleaned it, adjusted the stuck floats, and reinstalled the probe. All systems tested back to 100% operation.

problem: Two fueling points contained water in their sumps: RUL submersible turbine pump had a trace amount of water below the boots, and UDC 7/8 had approximately one-quarter of water below the boots.
solution: Technician removed 4 gallons of water from the sumps. Recommend investigating the source of water infiltration to prevent future contamination.

problem: Four diesel dispensers at fueling point 10 had restricted flow due to clogged filters.
solution: Technician replaced the diesel filter on fueling points 5 and 11, restoring full flow. Recommend keeping diesel filters in stock on-site to minimize downtime for future filter changes.

problem: An uninterruptible power supply (UPS) unit was not functioning properly, affecting the dispenser system.
solution: The technician replaced the failed UPS unit and recabled it with the Commander, FCI, Wayne connect, and Switch components. The system was verified to be operating normally after the replacement.

problem: Fueling point 12 universal hose nozzle was leaking near the bolt at the end of the nozzle handle.
solution: Technician repaired the swivel at the nozzle and tested all functions back to 100% operation.

problem: The lever switch on fueling point 3 was not functioning because the lever pin was broken, causing the nozzle lever to move freely with no tension and preventing fuel dispensing.
solution: The technician replaced the broken lever pin with a temporary paper clip substitute and tested the dispenser to confirm fuel dispensing was restored. A permanent replacement pin should be installed as soon as one becomes available.

problem: Fueling point 7 and fueling point 11 nozzle automatic shutoff was not engaging; fueling point 11 was taken out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and tested pump 3, pump 8, and pump 11 with no issues found. Technician witnessed three customers using the pump with no problems occurring. Equipment appears to be functioning normally.

problem: Fueling point 8 breakaway hose assembly was pulled off during a drive-off incident with no leaks or spills reported.
solution: Technician reattached the breakaway and nozzle assembly together, then tested pump 8 to confirm proper dispensing operation.

problem: All fuel dispenser grades were dispensing very slowly, one penny at a time.
solution: Technician changed the fuel filters on all dispensers after obtaining site approval. Verified each dispenser with test transactions to confirm they were operating at maximum flow capacity.

problem: Fueling point 5 dispenser screen was black and not displaying.
solution: Technician verified the display cable connection to the ANTX board was secure and properly seated, then performed a power cycle on the dispenser. Following the reboot, the screen functioned properly and the unit came online without errors.

problem: Fueling point 8 displayed a KSB error and was not accepting card payments due to a breached Secure Payment Module keypad housing.
solution: Replaced the Secure Payment Module keypad, injected a new debit key, configured the unit for operational mode, and verified the payment system with a successful test transaction.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x WAYNE Keypad Assembly, SPM2, U

problem: The 18-inch manhole cover near the kiosk window was broken.
solution: The technician arrived on site and replaced the 18-inch manhole lid. The site remained operational during the repair.

problem: Fueling point 12 uni-hose became disconnected at the nozzle swivel, taking the fueling point out of service.
solution: Technician reconnected the breakaway at the nozzle swivel and tested the pump twice to verify it was functioning properly and safe for customer use.

problem: Fueling point 2 displayed an 'Out of paper' error on the receipt printer even though paper was loaded, preventing customers from obtaining receipts.
solution: The technician inspected the printer, found and cleared a paper jam, cycled power to the dispenser, and tested the printer. The printer is now functioning correctly and prompting customers for receipts.

problem: Fueling point 2 printer is not printing receipts, not feeding paper, and not cutting, with no error messages displayed.
solution: The technician reconfigured the jade board correctly, which fixed the printer issue. The repair was tested and verified to be working.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispensers at the site had flow rates below 5 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced all 6 unleaded filters on the fueling points. All pumps were verified to be working properly after the filter replacement.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispenser had slow fuel flow.
solution: Replaced 6 fuel filters on the dispenser to restore normal flow rate.

problem: The nozzle breakaway on fueling point 4 was disconnected from the dispenser.
solution: The technician reconnected the breakaway to the dispenser, tested it to ensure full operation, and confirmed there were no leaks present.

problem: Fueling point 6 was offline and not operational.
solution: Technician brought fueling point 6 back online and verified it was running properly. Technician was instructed to confirm the latest software version was installed by comparing it against the current version available on the Wayne portal.

problem: Fueling point 3 card reader was blank and the fueling point was out of service.
solution: Technician tested fueling point 3 transaction and dispense functions on arrival and found them working with no issues. Card reader was tested under diagnostic and found to be good.

problem: Fueling point 3 was leaking at the swivel connection just below the nozzle and taken out of service.
solution: The technician retightened the swivel on dispenser 3 and tested all functions; the leak was resolved and the fueling point returned to full operation with no leaks present.

problem: Three fueling points (1/2, 3/4, and 7/8) each contained trace amounts of water in the sump below the pump boots.
solution: The technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sumps.

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose was leaking at the breakaway connection near the nozzle during use.
solution: Technician inspected the dispenser and swivel nozzle hose assembly but could not reproduce any leaks. The fueling point was returned to service with no signs of leaks detected. Possible customer error was noted. Issue covered under APEC warranty as dispenser was recently installed at the site.

problem: Fueling point 4 was offline with a frozen screen display.
solution: The technician cycled power to the dispenser to refresh the system, which restored all systems to normal operation.

problem: Fuel pump handle and nozzle assembly leaking at the swivel connection point.
solution: Replaced the nozzle, swivel, breakaway, and 6-inch whip hose. After replacement, the dispenser was pumping slowly, so the technician changed the filter and tested the unit, restoring full performance.

problem: Fueling point 3 was displaying a KSB error, indicating the SPM keypad was breached.

problem: All unleaded fueling points had flow rates below 5 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling point 7 was getting stuck on the 'remove card' message and would not proceed; rebooting the dispenser did not resolve the issue.
solution: Technician tested fueling point 7 on arrival and found it functioning normally with no problems detected in the card reader. Diagnostic mode testing also showed the equipment in good working condition, indicating the issue had resolved or was intermittent.

problem: Fueling point 10 was stuck on the Ovation boot load screen and unable to progress past startup; fueling point 11 was displaying a card reader unavailable message on the display screen.
solution: Technician inspected both fueling points and found FP11 was actually online and functioning normally—a test transaction completed successfully. FP10 was found to have a Jade board issue causing the boot load failure; technician attempted to debug the Jade board but notes do not indicate whether the issue was resolved or what the final outcome was.

problem: Fueling point 5 display was frozen showing today's date and 'SPM Operational' message, making the dispenser unusable.
solution: Technician reloaded the Jade board and system control device (SCD), completed the load and configuration process, and verified the fix with a successful test transaction.

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose was leaking from the breakaway connection, and fueling point 2 receipt printer was displaying 'Out of paper' error despite paper being present in the printer.
solution: The technician found that the breakaway fitting on fueling point 11 was loose and had no sealant; they applied pipe sealant and securely reattached the hose, then performed a successful pump test confirming no leaks. No issue was found with the receipt printer on fueling point 2.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 displayed black screens with no communication from the wireless in-dispenser unit.
solution: Performed a power cycle, completed an iXReset on the Jade board, and reloaded the software to restore screen functionality. Replaced the wireless in-dispenser unit, paired it to the access point, and initialized DCR/EMV on both fueling points; test transactions confirmed successful operation.

problem: Fueling point 2 keypad became unresponsive during operation, preventing debit card PIN entry, though the fueling point initially worked before the buttons stopped responding.
solution: Technician replaced the defective keypad and updated the software to restore full service. The fueling point is now operational.

problem: Fueling point 2 diesel hose nozzle is not sensing when the tank is full.
solution: Technician tested diesel on fueling points 1 and 2 and found no issue. Wave trip charge applied.

problem: Fueling points 5/6 and 11/12 were flashing 'Closed' and unable to dispense diesel fuel.
solution: Technician arrived on site and cleared the error that was causing the fueling points to display the closed status.

problem: Fueling point 11 printer had no power and would not print due to a broken power cable caused by someone pulling the printer too far forward.
solution: Technician replaced the printer power cable (part number WU009898-0001) going to the 24 volt power supply and also replaced the 24 volt power supply itself since it was no longer functioning. All systems tested good after repair.
parts: 1x WAYNE PCB Assembly, iGEM, 24V ; 1x WAYNE Cable Assembly, Zebra Pr

problem: Fueling point 6 has fuel leaking from the top, and fueling point 5 touchscreen is not responding to grade selection inputs.
solution: Technician contacted Wayne Helpdesk and gathered information about both issues by questioning the store operator and documenting the problems with photos of the ANTX board wiring, touch driver board, and both sides of fueling points 5 and 6, which were sent to the engineering team for further diagnosis.

problem: All tank probes on the TLS 300 tank monitor stopped working because the monitor lost its programming for all tanks.
solution: Reprogrammed the TLS 300 tank monitor and all probes returned to full operation. Technician noted the printer door cover is broken and causing an alarm on the tank monitor; a replacement printer door assembly is needed on the next service call. The premium probe occasionally shows low temperature and may need replacement in the future. The printer door is also slamming against the unit and causing the RS232 board to come loose, which will be corrected during the next visit when a new TLS 300 CPU board with correct software is installed.

problem: All fueling points were dispensing fuel very slowly (penny by penny) across all grades; filters had been changed approximately 3 months prior.
solution: Technician arrived on site, collected samples from the nozzles and filters of both regular tanks, and changed the filters on regular tank, which restored flow rate to 9-10 GPM. Technician needs to return to collect additional fuel samples from tank 1 and tank 2 regular (one from the middle and one from the bottom of each tank) to diagnose the underlying cause of the rapid filter degradation.

problem: The Integra 100 tank monitor was not reading any of the four ATG probes; troubleshooting revealed the regulator probe (serial number ending in 2032) was faulty and bringing down the entire daisy-chain circuit, and subsequently the monitor's CPU was found to be bad.
solution: Replaced all four probe cables and attempted to rewire the probes, but the monitor remained non-functional after the CPU was confirmed bad. The site needs to upgrade to a ProGauge monitor to resolve this issue. Contact Ron B. regarding warranty coverage for this repair.

problem: Preventative maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components (nozzles, swivels, hoses, breakaways, splash guards, and nozzle covers) at four fueling points to identify expired parts, damage, or components requiring replacement.
solution: Technician inspected all hanging hardware across four fueling points and replaced expired nozzles and swivels at fueling point 2 (nozzle expired 03/2027 replaced with 11/2030 expiration; swivel expired 02/2022 replaced with 09/2029 expiration) and nozzle cover at fueling point 2. Technician identified that whip hoses at fueling points 1, 2, and 3, eight-foot hoses at fueling points 3 and 4, and nozzle cover at fueling point 3 require ordering and replacement; these parts must be ordered and installed on a return visit per the preventative maintenance requirements.

solution: Opened the sump and found the STP dripping near the center of the motor head; checked the center adjustment screw and removed the check-valve housing, finding corrosion and a failed check-valve seal. Check valve and seal replacement needed to stop the leak.

problem: Pump 9 had a screen pixelation issue (Wayne warranty, Anthem display).

problem: Regular was intermittently shutting down (motor suspected, not under warranty), and FP 3/4 plus and premium were not working.
solution: Traced the regular fault to a contactor losing connection and replaced the contactor relay; on FP 3/4, re-landed the control wire in the main trough to energize the dispensers. All tested and working.
Found FP 7 in operation and watched customers use it without issue; all other dispensers operational. No fault found.

problem: A liquid alarm on the tank monitor (reported as the DEF STP sump, Sensor 7).
solution: Found it was actually the premium unleaded sump sensor with debris stuck in the bottom causing the alarm; cleaned and reinstalled the sensor - tank monitor back to 100%.

problem: Both registers were showing an MOP conflict error; FP 5 had a defective nozzle boot and FP 2/8 showed SPM KSB errors.
solution: Resolved the MOP conflict and replaced the defective FP 5 nozzle boot. Offered to replace the breached SPMs on FP 2/8, but the customer declined due to part cost.

problem: FP 6 was stopping mid-sale on regular grade (Anthem dispenser).
solution: Replaced the EBD board on FP 6 and tested.

problem: A pump motor needed replacement (the Guardian controller had been suspected).
solution: Changed out the Guardian controller and the station ran at 100%, but per the supervisor the in-ground motor should be replaced instead. Heavy rain prevented the in-ground work, so a return trip is required to replace the motor.

problem: Site needed a full software upgrade inside and out (Exxon, Ruby 2); on arrival FP 3/5/6/8 showed KSB errors and FP 2's screen was dim.
solution: Upgraded four iGem boards to firmware 85, the FP 1/2/4/7 Jade boards to 2.8.215, and the SPM firmware; upgraded the FP 4 contactless reader (the only one powered); and upgraded and reconfigured the Commander and both registers (LAN, network, EMV/rewards).
Checked dispensers 1/2/9/10 (8-10 GPM, fine); found 3/4 at about 3 GPM, replaced the filter and restored flow - station pumping within tolerance.

problem: FP 6's DCR screen was frozen on the store-hours display and dropped to 'IDPOS 10.3 Offline' after a couple of transactions.
solution: Reloaded both the SCD and Jade boards and reconfigured the Jade board to restore the card reader (test transactions had been failing).

problem: FP 9 and FP 12 were stuck in IDPOS (offline), and FP 10/11/12 had paper jams.

problem: Dispenser issue reported; specifics not recorded on the invoice.
solution: Not stated.
Powered FP 3/4 off at the breaker and restarted the dispenser; FP 4 came back, the price updated, and a $3 test (into a measuring can, returned to tank) ran with no issues.

problem: Diesel filters needed replacing on pumps 1/2.
solution: Replaced the diesel filter, purged and tested the line. Completed.

problem: Regular was running slow on pumps 1/2, plus a COIPA (contactless) error.
solution: Found the strainer completely clogged; cleaned it and installed a new filter, restoring 9-10 GPM. The COIPA error was the in-dispenser unit losing connection to the access point - refreshed the connection and the DCR came back.

problem: Helper visit - assisted another technician; no separate issue recorded.
solution: Not stated.

problem: Site needed a new EVO tank monitor installed (replacing an Incon), reusing the existing probes.

problem: Site needed a new tank gauge installed and set up.

problem: FP 4 had a detached breakaway from a drive-off, FP 7 diesel was not dispensing, and FP 3's customer display was not resetting.
solution: Replaced the FP 4 breakaway (damaged locking mechanism) and tested with no leaks; continued addressing FP 7 diesel and the FP 3 display per the reported issues (full resolution not recorded).

problem: Project installation - set up dual Topaz registers (Base 54 Chevron) and the full counter system.
solution: Installed and connected two Topaz registers, cash drawers, printers, scanners, a Mako router, a network switch and two M400 pinpads, running new Cat5/RS232 cabling. Noted the store's internet modem had no power (electrician to address the coax).

problem: Plus and premium were not pumping (reported as all pumps not working).
solution: Found loose connections keeping the plus/premium valves from opening and debris in the premium STP check valve that let fuel drain back and tripped the leak detector (its fitting had been removed by another company). Corrected the connections and check valve; the leak-detector fitting still needs to be restored.

problem: A failing nozzle on FP 3 and slow flow across FP 3/4 and FP 5/6.
solution: Replaced the FP 3 nozzle and replaced/purged the filters on FP 3/4 and the regular filter on FP 5/6, restoring normal flow (used on-site filters).
Reattached the breakaway and leak-tested (no leaks); pump-tested regular and premium at a steady 8 GPM with no slow flow.
Inspected the FP 8 printer and ran multiple print tests and a pump test - printed normally with no errors. No fault found.

problem: The premium solenoid on the B side of FP 3/4 had a damaged O-ring causing overflow and tripping iGem alarms; FP 1's nylon retractor cable was short and undone.
solution: Replaced the damaged solenoid and purged; replaced the dripping nozzle (damaged internal valve) and leak-tested. On FP 1, replaced the nylon cable and readjusted the spring-loaded disc.

solution: Not stated.

problem: Tank monitor problem (this invoice is a labor-credit adjustment for a prior 8/15 trip).
solution: Not stated - credited 3.88 of 5.98 labor hours from the 8/15 trip; no repair on this invoice.

problem: No card readers (DCRs) were online - pumps were down and customers had to pay inside.
solution: Found the Wayne Connect unit's Cat5 cable plugged into the wrong port on the Mako switch; moved it to COM port 7, powered up the Wayne Connect box, and initialized the DCRs from the register. Dispensers came back online.

problem: Both registers were slow to clear fuel prepays, which blocked new prepay fuel transactions (reported as diesel running slow).
solution: Routine steps (power cycle, port change, current-loop replacement) did not fix it, so reloaded and reconfigured the Commander and both registers (software, network, LAN, P97, loyalty). All tested and running normally.

problem: Dispenser issue (return trip; no detail recorded).
solution: Not stated.

problem: The site had two failed UPS battery backups.
solution: Replaced both UPS units to restore protected power. Completed.
Found no problem with the DCR (card reader); found one bad nozzle boot on FP 8 and replaced it. Tested.
Replaced the defective FP 5 nozzle and purged the line; watched a few customers fuel with no errors. Completed.

problem: An FP had a damaged breakaway, a skinned retractor cable and a badly damaged whip hose (likely a drive-off), plus slow flow.
solution: Replaced the hose and corrected the cable; the pump was running very slow, so replaced the filter, purged and tested - flow returned to 9-10 GPM. Also replaced the FP 5 nozzle boot.

problem: Reported hose/nozzle seal or gasket issue.

problem: The Fuelmaster unit needed a new keypad.

problem: Scheduled software upgrade (back up and upgrade the register).
solution: Found the new equipment and system had already been upgraded; double-checked and confirmed no issues with the current systems.

problem: FP 2 showed a KSB error (keypad/SPM fault).
solution: Replaced the SPM with a 2.5 model and injected keys; tested with a debit card - all functions normal.

problem: More than half the fueling positions were locking up during fuel price-change updates, forcing 3-4 pump resets a day; at arrival six units were unresponsive.
solution: Not stated - inspected the dispensers with the manager; the resolution was not recorded on the invoice.

problem: After a software upgrade, tapping a card returned a 'bad card reader' error on multiple cards (a known issue).
solution: Found the firmware matched but the build revision was below the required level; loaded a tap unit with the latest available and still got the error. Determined the problem is in the Chevron tables and needs a tables fix.

problem: FP 5's screen backlight was out due to a power connection issue, and FP 6 had a damaged whip hose.
solution: Adjusted the FP 5 power connection to restore the backlight; replaced the FP 6 whip hose and purged the line with no further leaks.

problem: A card reader had an IDPOS communication failure.
solution: Found the latest IDPOS software was missing crucial library data and would not load - a Dover software-download fix was needed. On the follow-up visit the issue had resolved itself.
Removed the damaged hose and purged the line; found no leaks and all functions normal.
Replaced the FP 4 SPM with a newer model, injected keys, and tested - all functions normal.

problem: A Chevron site's M400 pinpad was randomly losing power for hours at a time.
solution: Found the wrong (older MX700/MX800) power supply feeding the M400 through a UPS; swapped in the correct M400 power supply and the pinpad ran normally. Will return to replace the pinpad if it recurs.
Power-cycled the unit and confirmed it came back online. It is on software 10.B; the technician did not have the flash drive with the latest version for any further update.

problem: Site needed a software upgrade inside and outside (BP).
solution: Upgraded the Commander and both Topaz registers to BP Base 55 and configured loyalty and mobile payment (online and tested); upgraded eight Jade boards to 2.8.215 and updated some SPM firmware. All tested and running.

problem: Diesel was leaking and running slow on FP 5, 6 and 10 (FP 5/6 had major leaks, FP 10 a minor drip), plus a tank CSLD increase warning.
solution: Replaced the diesel nozzles on FP 5 and 6 and the FP 5/6 diesel filter; the slow flow was from disuse and air in the diesel side, so freed the stuck leak detector and restored both sides of pump 5/6 to full flow.
parts: 3x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" Leaded,11B, G [11B-0100] ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70065]

problem: FP 2 and FP 3 premium and midgrade were not pumping fuel (motor running but no product).
solution: Replaced a broken leak detector with a plug, but fuel still would not pump; a return trip is needed to further troubleshoot the premium STP (not a Wayne warranty issue).

problem: A dispenser/card software issue on FP 12.
solution: Reloaded the FP 12 Jade iX board with IDPOS (SCD auto-upgrade) and reconfigured; tested.

problem: The Veeder-Root TLS-450 tank monitor was offline.
solution: Rebooted the TLS-450 and everything came back online.

problem: FP 6 needed a new SPM and all the dispenser iGem boards needed a software upgrade.
solution: Injected and installed a new SPM on FP 6 (tested with a debit card), upgraded all iGem boards to Code 85 and configured them, and fixed some POS menu keys at the customer's request. All tested.

problem: Grade-select was not working on FP 6 and FP 14 (Wayne dispenser, warranty).
solution: Not stated. Return trip required to order two Anthem screens.

problem: Pump 21's card reader was stuck at 'Speedpass' and would not proceed; a prior contractor had broken the power cable from the SPM to the customer display.
solution: Determined Speedway needs to push a download to the reader; identified the broken SPM-to-display power cable, requiring a new customer display and possibly a new SPM. Speedway to coordinate.

problem: FP 11 would not dispense on prepaid transactions - products could be selected but nothing dispensed; diagnostics showed repeated 'RAM Totals CRC Error' on FP 11/12.
solution: Diagnosed a likely bad iGem dispenser computer (RAM CRC fault); a return trip is required to replace it.

problem: Pump 4 was rejecting network (card) transactions; the diagnostic host status showed offline.
solution: Determined the network cannot be recovered without a Commander reload (site is on Base 44 RubyCi with no MNSP router). The site manager will schedule the upgrade.

problem: Register 102's pinpad kept shutting off when a customer inserted a card.
solution: Found the pinpad's chip reader had failed; replaced the pinpad (under part warranty) and tested - all functions normal.

problem: Pumps were powered off and FP 7's regular hose was loose at the top (reported as a dispenser not running/leak).
solution: Tightened the loose FP 7 hose fitting, confirmed no further leaks, and cleaned the residue off the hose.
Verified proper voltage at the dispenser's power distribution board, then found the FP 7/8 breaker in the kiosk panel switched OFF; turned it back ON and the dispensers powered up and initialized.

solution: Replaced the FP 10 SPM and tested to confirm the issue resolved. Completed.
Found a breached SPM and replaced it with a new one, resolving the issue. Completed.

problem: Preventive maintenance on hanging hardware - hoses were dragging on the ground and components needed replacement.
solution: Adjusted all ground-touching hoses and reapplied the hose reels; replaced the breakaways on FP 11 and 12 and the hose, swivel and nozzle on FP 11 unleaded. Return visit required to finish.

problem: Site needed a new pinpad and SATADOM, and the Anthem dispenser screens on FP 5/6 were not responding.
solution: Installed the SATADOM in POS 2 and reloaded pinpad 2 (working). Reconnected the FP 5/6 Anthem screen connections and power-cycled, bringing FP 5/6 (and FP 14) back to normal operation.

problem: The register pinpad was not accepting debit cards (no DUKPT keys were applied), and the scanner was auto-scanning items without a trigger.
solution: Injected new debit (DUKPT) keys into both pinpads and confirmed debit now processes; reconfigured the scanner to 'trigger only' mode. A loyalty discount only applying on manual POS entry was also reported.
Downgraded the FP 3 Jade board software from 212 to 205, rebooted the Commander and both dispensers, re-initialized the DCR and DCR drive and EMV, and tested with a chip debit - all running normally.

problem: The printer door would not stay closed.
solution: Found the printer door lock loose; tightened it to fix the door and tested multiple times with the customer.

problem: FP 9's nozzle latch was not unlocking properly (the auto-shutoff itself worked).
solution: Replaced the FP 9 nozzle to prevent further issues; checked FP 10 for a similar problem and found its latch and auto-shutoff fine.

problem: The Fuelmaster diesel was not dispensing and the lever would not lift.
solution: Not stated.

problem: Water-detecting filters needed changing on FP 1/2 (other points checked), plus a worn regular grade-select button.
solution: Replaced the FP 1/2 filters, installed a customer-provided regular grade-select button and overlay, and calibrated all grades. Completed.

problem: Reported dispensing issue across several fueling points.
solution: Found only FP 2 affected - missing the auto-shutoff spring on regular; replaced the FP 2 nozzle. All other fueling points working as intended.

problem: FP 2's nozzle was missing its auto-shutoff spring, and FP 5 and FP 12 were offline showing 'Out of Order' with breached SPM keypads.
solution: Replaced the FP 2 nozzle; replaced both breached SPM keypads on FP 5 and FP 12, injected the debit key, returned them to operational mode, rebooted and tested online.

problem: Systems were down - the Veeder-Root breaker had tripped.

problem: FP 10's breakaway was leaking and its nozzle spout was damaged.

problem: A newly installed Progauge tank gauge showed fuel inches but not volume, and on arrival no grades were pumping.
solution: Verified motor capacitors, motors and STP voltages were within tolerance (~80V on leads); found a bad premium STP cable on pump 3/4 and contactors not fully engaging. Further troubleshooting required; the gauge to be reconfigured once the site has fuel.
Not stated.

problem: Construction/install task - the regular sump sensor was out of position.

problem: Construction/install task on the premium tank.

solution: Tested FP 4 and FP 2 tap - both processed normally (the failures traced to one customer's card). Reset the FP 3 printer's paper alarm and it printed normally. No equipment fault found.

problem: The tank monitor (Incon Integra) was not reading the regular or premium probes - all signals lost.
solution: Tested the daisy-chained probes channel by channel and found one of four channels blown. A return trip with an ISBL barrier board is required to locate and clear the source.

problem: FP 11 and 12 were pumping slowly; FP 12 was worse than normal.

problem: The diesel tank probe was not functioning and there was 3 inches of water in the diesel tank with debris and buildup inside.
solution: Removed water from the sump, replaced the non-functioning probe with a new 10-foot probe with diesel float kit, and tested all functions which returned to normal. Tank cleaning is recommended to address the debris and buildup inside the tank.
parts: 1x Probe 10 ft Mag2 "SS" ; 1x FLOAT KIT 4" MAG PLUS 10" CB

solution: Changed tank level sensor setup from weekly Saturday inspections to daily morning inspections.

problem: Customer requested calibration on two dispensers; during the visit the regular grade on FP 1 stopped working.
solution: Calibrated FP 5 regular/plus and FP 6 regular. Cross-tested grades and diagnosed FP 1 regular as a bad valve - return with a valve to complete.
